DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication whether or not data were collected for the intervention topic.
Question Text: Has the subject had any [intervention topic(s)] (after/before) [study-specific time frame] (after/before [study-specific time frame] )?; [Was/Were] (there) any [intervention topic(s)] [taken/performed/used/collected] (after/before) [study-specific time frame]?
Mapping Instructions: Does not map to an SDTM variable. The SDTM annotated CRF indicates that this field is NOT SUBMITTED.
Implementation Notes: General prompt question to aid in monitoring and data cleaning. This provides verification that all other fields on the CRF were deliberately left blank. This is a field that can be used on any Interventions CRF to indicate whether or not there are data to record.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The topic for the intervention observation, usually the reported name of the treatment, drug, medicine, or therapy given during the dosing interval for the observation.
Question Text: What [is/was] the (type of) [treatment/investigational product/intervention topic]?; [If Other is selected], [explain/specify/provide more details].
Prompt: [Treatment/Investigational Product/Intervention Name]; [Specify Other/Explain/Specify Details] [Treatment/Investigational Product/Intervention Name]
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: If --TRT is preprinted/ prespecified, the value should also be mapped into the --TRT variable (e.g., if Oral Steroid is preprinted on a CM CRF, "Oral Steroid" should be stored in CMTRT). The CDASH field --TRT can also be used to collect any free-text values linked to the sponsor standardized value collected in the CDASH field --DECOD. For example, the CDASH field --DECOD may have a value of "OTHER" and the associated free-text intervention topic is collected in the CDASH field --TRT. In this scenario, the Item prompt "Specify Other" may be used.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The dictionary or sponsor-defined standardized text description of the topic variable, --TRT, or the modified topic variable (-- MODIFY), if applicable.
Question Text: What [is/ was] the [treatment/intervention topic]?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: When populated by a coding dictionary (e.g., WHO Drug, MedDRA), Question Text and Prompt are not applicable. WHO Drug may be used for coding treatment names and MedDRA may be used for coding procedures. When these dictionaries are used, --DECOD is equivalent to those dictionaries' Preferred Term. The CDASH field --DECOD may be used to collect standardized prespecified values (CDISC Controlled Terminology or sponsor-defined) on a CRF. The CDASH field --TRT can be used to collect any free-text values linked to the sponsor standardized value. For example, the CDASH field --DECOD may have a value of "OTHER" and the associated free-text intervention topic is collected in CDASH field --TRT.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The mode or condition of the record that specifies whether the intervention (activity) is intended to happen or has happened.
Question Text: Does this record describe scheduled treatment or performed treatment?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: "SCHEDULED" is used when collecting subject-level intended dose records. "PERFORMED" is used when collecting subject-level actual dose records. This would most commonly be a heading on the CRF and not a question to which the site would provide an answer. If collecting both scheduled and performed dosing in the same horizontal record, the sponsor may append "_SCHEDULED" to the variable name to capture the scheduled dose.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A grouping of topic-variable values based on user-defined characteristics.
Question Text: What [is/was] the category (of the [intervention])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Sponsor-defined controlled terminology. This would most commonly be a heading on the CRF or screen, not a question to which the site would provide an answer. If a question is asked, the response would typically come from a sponsor-defined codelist. If the form is laid out as a grid, then words such as "Category" can be included as the column header. See examples in the CDASHIG metadata table and in the SDTMIG.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A subdivision of the --CAT values based on user-defined characteristics.
Question Text: What [is/was] the subcategory (of the [intervention])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Sponsor-defined controlled terminology. This would most commonly be a heading on the CRF or screen, not a question to which the site would provide an answer. --SCAT can only be used if there is a --CAT. If a question is asked, the response would typically come from a sponsor-defined codelist. If the form is laid out as a grid, then words such as "Subcategory" can be included as the column header. See examples in the CDASHIG metadata table and in the SDTMIG.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication that a specific intervention or a group of interventions is prespecified on a CRF.
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: For prespecified interventions, a hidden field on a CRF may be defaulted to Y, or added during SDTM dataset creation. If a study collects both prespecified and free-text interventions, the value of --PRESP should be "Y" for all prespecified interventions and null for interventions reported as free text.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication that the prespecified intervention happened or was administered when information about the occurrence of the specific intervention is solicited.
Question Text: Was [--TRT/ intervention] [taken/performed/administered/consumed] (after/before [study-specific time frame])?;Has the subject [had/taken/performed/administered/consumed] the [--TRT/ intervention]?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Used for specific interventions that are collected as defined by the protocol. If the term is preprinted/prespecified, the value should also be mapped into the --TRT variable (e.g., if Oral Steroid is preprinted on a CM CRF, "Oral Steroid" should be stored in CMTRT). The CDASH field --OCCUR is not used for spontaneously free-text reported --TRT. Should not be used to indicate data were not collected. Used only when the value can be collected using values from the CDISC CT (NY) codelist. --OCCUR is a Yes/No variable and in the SDTM submission datasets, --OCCUR is populated only for the records where --PRESP is "Y" and is null when --PRESP is null.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The variable used to indicate whether data are available, by having the site recording the value as "Yes" or "No".
Question Text: (Were/Was) (the) [intervention topic] [answered/done/assessed/evaluated/available]?
Mapping Instructions: This field does not map directly to an SDTM variable. May be used to populate a value into the SDTM variable --STAT to indicate when a prespecified Intervention was not assessed. If the CDASH variable --PERF="N", the value of the STDM variable --STAT is "NOT DONE". If --PERF= "Y", --STAT is null.
Implementation Notes: Using --PERF, a negative response can be collected as "N" and mapped to the --STAT variable in SDTM as "NOT DONE". --PERF can be used instead of --STAT when a Y/N response list is needed for implementation. Examples: Were prior medications assessed? Were medications of interest assessed? \n

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The variable used to indicate that data are not available, by having the site recording the value as "Not Done".
Question Text: Was the [intervention topic] not [answered/done/assessed/evaluated/available]?; Indicate if ([intervention topic] was) not [answered/done/assessed/evaluated/available].
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column. If collected, the Origin (column in the Define-XML) = "CRF"; if populated from other sources such as a free text or sponsor-defined listing for --REASND, the Origin ="DERIVED".
Implementation Notes: The value of "Not Done" indicates either that data are not available or the question was not asked. Do not use this CDASH field when collecting information on whether or not a prespecified intervention was performed; this should be recorded using the CDASH field --OCCUR.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An explanation of why data are not available.
Question Text: What [is/was] the reason that the [Interventions topic/data/information/sponsor-defined phrase] was not [collected/answered/done/assessed/evaluated/available]?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Sponsor-defined controlled terminology may be used. The reason the data are not available may be chosen from a sponsor-defined codelist (e.g., broken equipment, subject refused) or entered as free text. When --REASND is used, --STAT should also be populated in the SDTM dataset.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The condition, disease, symptom, or disorder that the intervention was used to address or investigate (e.g., why the therapy was taken or administered, why the procedure was performed).
Question Text: For what indication was the [--TRT] [taken/performed/administered/consumed]?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: This additional information is collected on the CRF when the sponsor wants to capture the indication(s)/medical problem(s) why a subject took/had an intervention. This information can then be used as deemed appropriate for coding, analysis, or for reconciling the interventions as part of the data clean-up and monitoring process.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The amount of substance (e.g., --TRT) given at one time, represented as a numeric value.
Question Text: What [is/ was] the (individual) (intended) [dose/amount] (of [--TRT] [taken/performed/administered/consumed/per administration])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Used when the dose/amount taken/administered/consumed has only numeric entries. If non-numeric entries are possible, use the CDASH field --DSTXT.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The amount of substance (e.g., --TRT) given at one time, represented in text format.
Question Text: What [is/was] the (individual) (intended) [dose/amount] (of [--TRT] [taken/performed/administered/consumed/per administration])?
Mapping Instructions: Does not directly map to SDTM. Maps to either --DOSE or DOSTXT.
Implementation Notes: Defining this data collection field as a dose text field allows for flexibility in capturing dose entries as numbers, text, or ranges.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The unit for --DOSE, --DOSTOT, or --DOSTXT.
Question Text: What [is/was] the unit (for the dose/amount of [--TRT])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: When possible, this is preprinted on the CRF with the associated treatment. Dose unit may be derived via other methods (e.g., from protocol, from data). When collected, the unit is preprinted on the CRF or a field is provided on the CRF to capture it.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The form in which --TRT is physically presented.
Question Text: What [is/was] the dose form (of the [--TRT])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The number of doses given/administered/taken during a specific interval.
Question Text: What [is/was] the frequency (of the [--TRT])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: When possible, the options for dose/amount frequency are preprinted on the CRF. When collected, the recommendation is to collect dosing information in separate fields (e.g., --DOSE ,--DOSEU,--DOSFRQ) for specific and consistent data collection and to enable programmatically utilizing these data.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The total amount of --TRT taken over a day, using the units in --DOSU.
Question Text: What [is/was] the total [dose/amount] (of the [--TRT/Intervention] [taken/performed/administered/consumed])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Used when dosing is collected as total daily dose

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The text description of the intended dosing schedule for the administration of the intervention.
Question Text: What [is/was] the intended dose regimen (of the [--TRT])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: When possible, the options for intended dose regimen are preprinted on the CRF. The sponsor may wish to create a codelist to collect this data consistently. Example: TWO WEEKS ON, TWO WEEKS OFF.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The route of administration of the intervention.
Question Text: What [is/was] the route of administration (of the [--TRT])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Lot number of the intervention product.
Question Text: What [is/was] the lot number (of the [--TRT])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: The lot number identifies the manufacturing batch of the intervention product. In open-label studies, the reference number on the product container may represent an actual lot number and should be submitted using --LOT. This variable may be populated during the process of creating the SDTM submission datasets. Do not collect other identification variables in this field.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The anatomical location of an intervention (e.g., injection site).
Question Text: What [is/was] the anatomical location (of the administration/where the [intervention] was taken/performed)?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: This may be preprinted or collected. --LOC is used only to specify the anatomical location. --LAT, --DIR, --PORTOT are used to further describe the anatomical location.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Qualifier for anatomical location further detailing side of intervention administration.
Question Text: What [is/was] the side (of the anatomical location of the administration)?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Further detailing the laterality of the location where the --TRT was administered/taken. This may be preprinted or collected. Sponsors may collect the data using a subset list of CT on the CRF.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Qualifier further detailing the position of the anatomical location relative to the center of the body, organ, or specimen.
Question Text: What [is/was] the directionality (of the anatomical location of the administration)?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Further detailing the directionality of the location where the --TRT was administered/taken (e.g., ANTERIOR, LOWER, PROXIMAL). This may be preprinted or collected. Sponsors may collect the data using a subset list of CT on the CRF.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Qualifier for anatomical location further detailing the distribution (i.e., arrangement of, apportioning of).
Question Text: What [is/was] the portion or totality (of the anatomical location of the administration)?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Further detailing the portion or totality of the location where the --TRT was administered/taken. This may be preprinted or collected.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication that the subject has abstained from food/water for the specified amount of time.
Question Text: [Is/was] the subject fasting (prior to study treatment [being taken/administered/given])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The amount of an active ingredient expressed quantitatively per dosage unit, per unit of volume, or per unit of weight, according to the pharmaceutical dose form.
Question Text: What [is/was] the pharmaceutical strength (of the [--TRT])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The unit for --PSTRG.
Question Text: What [is/was] the unit (of the pharmaceutical strength (of the [--TRT]))?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: The unit is preprinted on the CRF or a field is provided on the CRF to capture it.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The vehicle for administration of treatment (e.g., a liquid in which the treatment drug is dissolved).
Question Text: What [is/was] the vehicle for administration of the [--TRT]?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The amount of the prepared product (treatment + vehicle) administered or given.
Question Text: What [is/was] the amount (of the prepared product (treatment + vehicle) [administered/taken])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Note: Should not be diluent amount alone

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The unit of measurement for the prepared product (treatment + vehicle).
Question Text: What [is/was] the unit?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: The unit is preprinted on the CRF or a field is provided on the CRF to capture it.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The flow rate for the total amount of drug + vehicle administered to the subject.
Question Text: What [is/was] the ([--TRT] infusion) rate?
Mapping Instructions: This information could be submitted in a SUPP-- dataset as the value of SUPP--.QVAL where SUPP--.QNAM = "-- FLRT" and SUPP--.QLABEL = "Infusion Rate". Refer to the current SDTM and SDTMIG for instructions on placement of non-standard variables (NSVs) in SDTM domains.
Implementation Notes: Infusion rate can be used to derive dose.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The unit of measure for the flow rate for the total amount of drug + vehicle administered to the subject.
Question Text: What [is/was] the ([--TRT] infusion rate) unit?
Mapping Instructions: This information could be submitted in a SUPP-- dataset as the value of SUPP--.QVAL where SUPP--.QNAM= "--FLRTU" and SUPP.QLABEL= "Infusion Rate Unit". Refer to the current SDTM and SDTMIG for instructions on placement of NSVs in SDTM domains.
Implementation Notes: The infusion rate unit (e.g., mL/min) is preprinted on the CRF or a field is provided on the CRF to capture it.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Description of or explanation for why a dose/amount of the intervention is adjusted.
Question Text: What [is/was] the reason the (study treatment/procedure) [dose/amount] was adjusted?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: The implementer may choose to create sponsor-defined controlled terminology (e.g., Adverse Event, Insufficient Response, Nonmedical Reason).

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication whether or not the dose was adjusted.
Question Text: [Is/was] the (study treatment/procedure) [dose/amount] adjusted?
Mapping Instructions: Does not map to an SDTM variable. The SDTM annotated CRF indicates that this field is NOT SUBMITTED. If the Reason for Dose Adjustment is not collected, the sponsor might chose to submit this as a supplemental qualifier.
Implementation Notes: Typically, the intent/purpose of collecting this field is to help with data cleaning and monitoring. It provides verification that the associated field on the CRF was deliberately left blank. However, the sponsor may collect whether the intervention was adjusted, without collecting the reason for the change.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication whether or not the intervention was interrupted.
Question Text: [Is/was] the [--TRT/Intervention] interrupted?
Mapping Instructions: Does not map to an SDTM variable. The SDTM annotated CRF indicates that this field is NOT SUBMITTED.
Implementation Notes: The intent/purpose of collecting this field is to help with data cleaning and monitoring when the actual duration of the interruption is collected using the CDASH field --CINTD. In some situations, if the actual duration of the interruption is not collected, or not derived, this information could be submitted in a SUPP--.QVAL dataset where SUPP--.QNAM = "--ITRPYN" and SUPP--.QLABEL = "Intervention Interrupted".

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An explanation for why the scheduled intervention did or did not occur.
Question Text: What [is/was] the reason the [intervention topic] was [(not) [performed/taken/done/administered]]? What [is/was] the reason for the [intervention topic] occur value?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: The reason the intervention did or did not occur may be chosen from a sponsor-defined codelist (e.g., SUBJECT MISTAKE, SUBJECT REFUSED) or entered as free text. When --REASOC is used, --OCCUR must also be populated.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication why the intervention was interrupted.
Question Text: Why was the [--TRT/Intervention] interrupted?
Mapping Instructions: This information could be submitted in a SUPP-- dataset as the value of SUPP--.QVAL where SUPP--.QNAM = "--ITRPRS" and SUPP--.QLABEL ="Reason Intervention Interrupted". Refer to the current SDTM and SDTMIG for instructions on placement of NSVs in SDTM domains.
Implementation Notes: This CDASH field is use to collected the reason an intervention was interrupted. Sponsors may define their own controlled terminology.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The collected duration of the intervention interruption.
Question Text: What was the duration of the [--TRT/Intervention] interruption?; How long was the [--TRT/Intervention] interruption?
Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTM variable. This information could be submitted in a SUPP-- dataset as the value of SUPP--.QVAL where SUPP--.QNAM = "--ITRPD" and SUPP--.QLABEL= "Interruption Duration". Concatenate the collected intervention interruption duration and the duration unit components and create --ITRPD using ISO 8601 period format. Refer to the current SDTM and SDTMIG for instructions on placement of NSVs in SDTM domains.
Implementation Notes: This CDASH field is use to collected the duration of an intervention interruption. In some situations, the duration of the interruption may not be collected but calculated from the intervention start and end times recorded elsewhere in the CRF.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The unit for the collected duration of intervention interruption.
Question Text: What [is/was] the (interruption duration) unit?
Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTM variable. This information could be submitted in a SUPP-- dataset as the value of SUPP--.QVAL where SUPP--.QNAM = "--ITRPD" and SUPP--.QLABEL= "Interruption Duration". Concatenate the collected intervention interruption duration and the duration unit components and create --ITRPD using ISO 8601 Period format. Refer to the current SDTM and SDTMIG for instructions on placement of NSVs in SDTM domains.
Implementation Notes: The unit is preprinted on the CRF or a field is provided on the CRF to capture it.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication of whether or not the subject completed the intended regimen.
Question Text: Did the subject complete [treatment/ the full course/sponsor provided phrase] (of the [--TRT/Intervention])?
Mapping Instructions: This information could be submitted in a SUPP-- dataset as the value of SUPP--.QVAL where SUPP--.QNAM = "--TRTCMP" and SUPP--.QLABEL ="Completed Treatment". Refer to the current SDTM and SDTMIG for instructions on placement of NSVs in SDTM domains.
Implementation Notes: This could be used when treatment is given as a fixed number of cycles and the sponsor needs a flag to indicate that the treatment has been completed as planned.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication of whether the substance was used and when it was used.
Question Text: Has the subject ever [used/consumed] [--TRT/Intervention]?
Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTM variable. May be used to populate a value into the SDTM variable --OCCUR. If --NCF = "Never", the value of --OCCUR will be "N". If --NCF = "Current" or "Former", the value of --OCCUR will be "Y". May also be used to populate a value into an SDTM relative timing variable such as --STRTPT or --STRF. If the value of --NCF is "Former", the value of ""BEFORE" may be used. If the value of --NCF is "current", the value of "ONGOING" may be used. When populating --STRTPT, if the value of --NCF is "Former", the value of "BEFORE" may be used. Note: --STRTPT must refer to a time point anchor described in --STTPT. This variable may also be used to populate the SDTM ending relative timing variables.
Implementation Notes: The preferred SDTM mapping is provided. This information is usually not submitted in a SUPP--.QVAL dataset.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication whether the intervention is ongoing as of a given timepoint when no end date is provided.
Question Text: [Is/Was] the [--TRT/Intervention] ongoing (as of [the study-specific timepoint or period])?
Prompt: Ongoing (as of the [Study-Specific Timepoint or Period])
Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTM variable. May be used to populate a value into an SDTM relative timing variable such as --ENRF or --ENRTPT. When populating --ENRF, or --ENRTPT, if the value of the CDASH field --ONGO is "Y" a value from the CDISC CT (STENRF) may be used. When --ONGO refers to the Study Reference Period (defined in DM.RFSTDTC to DM.RFENDTC), the SDTM variable --ENRF should be populated. When --ONGO is compared to another timepoint, the SDTM variables --ENRTPT and --ENTPT should be used. Note: --ENRTPT must refer to the time point anchor described in --ENTPT.
Implementation Notes: The CDASH field --ONGO allows specific question text/prompt about interventions ending after the study or after a given timepoint. Select the appropriate text when designing the CRF. This may also be included in the CRF title or instructions. Used in conjunction with either a reference timepoint (--ENTPT, --ENRTPT) or in conjunction with the Study Reference Period (described as RFSTDTC to RFENDTC). May also be used as a tick/checkbox. See CDASHIG Section 3.7 for more information.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A free-text comment.
Question Text: [Protocol-specified Targeted Question]?
Prompt: [Abbreviated version of the protocol-specified targeted question]
Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTM variable. For the SDTM dataset, the CDASH variable --COVAL maps to the SDTM variable COVAL (COVAL2, COVAL3) in the CO domain. Associate the free-text comment in the CO domain with the original record using RDOMAIN, IDVAR, IDVARVAL, COREF.
Implementation Notes: If an additional free-text field is needed to provide a comment about a particular record, use the COVAL(n) field to collect the free text, and associate the free-text comment with the original record using SDTM variables RDOMAIN, IDVAR, IDVARVAL, COREF. See the SDTMIG for more information.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: If the value for --TRT is modified for coding purposes, then the modified text is placed here.
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: This is not a data collection field that will appear on the CRF. Sponsors will populate this through the coding process. Rationale for adding coding variables to CDASH is to provide a more complete data management package.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The class for the intervention, often obtained from a coding dictionary.
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column. The sponsor is expected to provide the dictionary name and version used to code interventions, using Define-XML external codelist attributes. Sponsors should include an Origin column in the define metadata document to indicate that the data was "ASSIGNED".
Implementation Notes: This is not a data collection field that will appear on the CRF itself. Sponsors will populate this through the coding process (e.g., WHO Drug). This would generally be the class used for analysis.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The assigned dictionary code for the class for the intervention.
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column. The sponsor is expected to provide the dictionary name and version used to code interventions,, using Define-XML external codelist attributes. Sponsors should include an Origin column in the define metadata document to indicate that the data was "ASSIGNED".
Implementation Notes: This is not a data collection field that will appear on the CRF itself. Sponsors will populate this through the coding process (e.g., WHO Drug). This would generally be the class used for analysis.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The way in which the event, visit, or contact was conducted.
Question Text: What was the contact mode?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication whether the intervention was changed due to an epidemic or pandemic.
Question Text: Was the [intervention/treatment] changed due to an epidemic or pandemic?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The reason the intervention was discontinued.
Question Text: What was the reason the [intervention/treatment] was discontinued?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The first level of hierarchy within the Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical (ATC) Classification System; indicates the anatomical main group.
Mapping Instructions: This information could be submitted in a SUPP-- dataset as the value of SUPP--.QVAL where SUPP--.QNAM= "--ATC1" and SUPP--QLABEL= "ATC Level 1 Description". Refer to the current SDTM and SDTMIG for instructions on placement of NSVs in SDTM domains. Sponsors should include an Origin column in the SUPPQ dataset to indicate that the data was "ASSIGNED".
Implementation Notes: This is not a data collection field that will appear on the CRF itself. Sponsors will populate this through the coding process (e.g., WHO Drug). Rationale for adding coding variables to CDASH is to provide a more complete data management package. This is an example of variables that could be used in the CM domain for coding. Other dictionaries can be used and the appropriate coding elements added, if appropriate, in the SUPP--.QVAL dataset.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The assigned dictionary code denoting the first level of hierarchy within the ATC Classification System; indicates the anatomical main group.
Mapping Instructions: This information could be submitted in a SUPP--dataset as the value of SUPP--.QVAL where SUPP--.QNAM = "--ATC1CD" and SUPP--.QLABEL="ATC Level 1 Code". Refer to the current SDTM and SDTMIG for instructions on placement of NSVs in SDTM domains. Sponsors should include an Origin column in the SUPPQ dataset to indicate that the data was "ASSIGNED".
Implementation Notes: This is not a data collection field that will appear on the CRF itself. Sponsors will populate this through the coding process (e.g., WHO Drug). Rationale for adding coding variables to CDASH is to provide a more complete data management package. This is an example of variables that could be used in the CM domain for coding. Other dictionaries can be used and the appropriate coding elements added, if appropriate, in the SUPP--.QVAL dataset.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The second level of hierarchy within the ATC Classification System; indicates the therapeutic main group.
Mapping Instructions: This information could be submitted in a SUPP-- dataset as the value of SUPP--.QVAL where SUPP--.QNAM = --ATC2 and SUPP--QLABEL="ATC Level 2 Description". Refer to the current SDTM and SDTMIG for instructions on placement of NSVs in SDTM domains. Sponsors should include an Origin column in the SUPPQ dataset to indicate that the data was "ASSIGNED".
Implementation Notes: This is not a data collection field that will appear on the CRF itself. Sponsors will populate this through the coding process (e.g., WHO Drug). Rationale for adding coding variables to CDASH is to provide a more complete data management package. This is an example of variables that could be used in the CM domain for coding. Other dictionaries can be used and the appropriate coding elements added, if appropriate, in the SUPP--.QVAL dataset.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A description of the active ingredients used in the medication taken or administered.
Question Text: What [are/were] the active ingredients?
Mapping Instructions: This information could be submitted in a SUPP--.QVAL dataset where SUPP--.QNAM = "--INGRID" and SUPP--.QLABEL= "Active Ingredients". Refer to the current SDTM and SDTMIG for instructions on placement of NSVs in SDTM domains.
Implementation Notes: This may be collected in addition to the medication/therapy name. Collecting this provides more detailed information when coding to a dictionary (e.g., WHO Drug Enhanced C Format now codes to the ingredient level for many brand-name medications).

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication whether or not any data were collected for the event topic.
Question Text: Has the subject had any [event topic(s)/term/name] ([study specific time frame])?; [Was/Were] (there) any [event topic(s)] (reported) ([study specific time frame])?
Mapping Instructions: Does not map to an SDTM variable. The SDTM annotated CRF indicates that this field is NOT SUBMITTED.
Implementation Notes: This is a field that can be used on any Events CRF to indicate whether or not there are data to record. Used primarily as a data cleaning field, this provides verification that all other fields on the CRF were deliberately left blank. If the response to YES/NO is planned to be submitted in SDTM submission datasets, this CDASH variable should not be used and the appropriate CDASH variable should be used instead (e.g., --OCCUR).

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The topic variable for an event observation, which is the reported or prespecified name of the event.
Question Text: What [is/was] the [event topic/term/name]?; If --DECOD (is selected), [explain/specify/provide (more) detail(s)]?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Typically, --TERM collects the verbatim text for an event. If the term is preprinted/prespecified, the value can be populated into the --TERM variable as a hidden field (e.g., if Headache is printed on an AE CRF, "Headache" should be stored in AETERM). The CDASH field --TERM can also be used to collect any free-text values linked to the sponsor-standardized value collected in the CDASH field --DECOD. For example, --DECOD may have a value of "OTHER" and the associated free-text event topic is collected in --TERM. In this scenario, the Item prompt "Specify Other" may be used.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The dictionary or sponsor-defined standardized text description of the topic variable; --TERM; or the modified topic variable (-- MODIFY), if applicable.
Question Text: What [is/was] the [event/event topic] (at the EPOCH/study specific time frame)?
Prompt: (Standardized) [Event/Event Topic] (at the EPOCH/Study Specific Time Frame)
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column. If sponsors use a dictionary to code --TERM, they should provide the dictionary name and version in the Define-XML external codelist attributes. Sponsors should include an Origin column in the define metadata document to indicate that the data was "ASSIGNED".
Implementation Notes: When populated by a coding dictionary (e.g., MedDRA), Question Text and Prompt may not be applicable. This is equivalent to the Preferred Term (PT) in MedDRA. CDISC Controlled terminology (NCOMPLT) is used for the DS domain only. The CDASH field --DECOD may be used to collect standardized pre-specified values (CDISC Controlled Terminology or sponsor-defined terminology) on a CRF. The CDASH field --TERM can be used to collect any free-text values linked to the sponsor-standardized value. For example, the CDASH field --DECOD may have a value of "OTHER" and the associated free-text event topic is collected in the CDASH field --TERM.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A grouping of topic-variable values based on user-defined characteristics.
Question Text: What [is/was] the category (of the [--TERM/event topic])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Sponsor-defined controlled terminology. This would most commonly be a heading on the CRF or screen, not a question to which the site would provide an answer. --SCAT can only be used if there is --CAT. If a question is asked, the response would typically be a sponsor-defined codelist. If the form is laid out as a grid, then words such as "category" can be included as the column header. Examples are provided in the CDASHIG metadata table and in the SDTMIG.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A subdivision of the --CAT values based on user-defined characteristics.
Question Text: What [is/was] the subcategory (of the [--TERM/event topic])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Sponsor-defined controlled terminology. This would most commonly be a heading on the CRF or screen, not a question to which the site would provide an answer. --SCAT can only be used if there is --CAT. See examples in the CDASHIG metadata table and in the SDTMIG.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication that a specific event, or group of events, is prespecified on a CRF.
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: For prespecified events, --PRESP is a hidden field on a CRF defaulted to Y, or can be added during SDTM dataset creation. If a study collects both prespecified and free-text events, the value of --PRESP should be "Y" for all prespecified events and null for events reported as free text.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication whether the prespecified event or the group of events occurred when the occurrence of the specific event or group of events is solicited.
Question Text: [Did/Does/Was] the subject (have) [--TERM] (after/before [study-specific time frame]) ?; [Did/Is] (the) [pre-specified --TERM/--DECOD] [occur/occurring]?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: If the term is preprinted/prespecified, then the value is mapped into the --TERM variable (e.g., if Headache is printed on an AE CRF, "Headache" should be stored in AETERM).

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An explanation for why the event topic did or did not occur.
Question Text: What [is/was] the reason the [event topic] [did/did not] [occur/happen]? What [is/was] the reason for the [event topic] occur value?
Prompt: Reason [Event Topic] [(Did/Did Not) Occur]; Reason For Occur Value
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: The reason the event did or did not occur may be chosen from a sponsor-defined codelist (e.g., SUBJECT MISTAKE, SUBJECT REFUSED) or entered as free text. When --EASOC is used, --OCCUR must also be populated with a value of "N". Not used in the in AE domain.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The variable used to indicate whether data are available, by having the site record the value as "Yes" or "No".
Question Text: (Were/Was) (the) [event topic] [answered/done/assessed/evaluated/available]?
Mapping Instructions: This field does not map directly to an SDTM variable. May be used to populate a value into the SDTM variable --STAT to indicate when a pre-specified event was not assessed. For use with pre-specified events, when the CDASH variable --PERF="N", the value of the STDM variable --STAT is "NOT DONE". If --PERF= "Y", --STAT is null.
Implementation Notes: Using --PERF, a response of "Y" or "N" can be collected. A negative response can be collected as "N" and mapped to the --STAT variable in SDTM as "NOT DONE". --PERF can be used instead of --STAT when a Y/N response list is needed for implementation. Example: Were medical history conditions assessed?

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The variable used to indicate that data are not available, by having the site record the value as "Not Done".
Question Text: Was the [event topic] not [answered/done/assessed/evaluated]?; Indicate if ([event topic] was) not [answered/assessed/done/evaluated].
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column. If collected on the CRF, the Origin (column in the Define-XML) = "CRF"; if populated by the sponsor from other sources (e.g., free text, a sponsor-defined listing for --REASND), Origin = "DERIVED".
Implementation Notes: A Not Done checkbox indicates the data are not available/question was not asked. Typically, there would be 1 checkbox for each prespecified event. This field can be useful when multiple questions are asked to confirm that a blank result field is intended to be blank.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An explanation for why the assessment/evaluation/question was not answered, collected, or done.
Question Text: What [is/was] the reason that the ([data/information/sponsor-defined phrase]) was not [answered/collected/done/evaluated/assessed]?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Sponsor-defined controlled terminology may be used. The reason the data was not done/not collected may be chosen from a select list (e.g., broken equipment, subject refused) or entered as free text. When --REASND is used, --STAT should also be populated in the SDTM dataset.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A description of the anatomical location relevant for the event.
Question Text: What [is/was] the anatomical location (of the [--TERM/event topic])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: This may be preprinted or collected. --LOC is used only to specify the anatomical location. --LAT, --DIR, --PORTOT are used to further describe the anatomical location.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Qualifier for anatomical location, further detailing the side of the body relevant for the event.
Question Text: What [is/was] the side (of the anatomical location of the event)?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Further detailing the laterality of the location of the --TERM. This may be preprinted or collected. Sponsors may collect the data using a subset list of CT on the CRF.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Qualifier further detailing the position of the anatomical location relative to the center of the body, organ, or specimen.
Question Text: What [is/was] the directionality (of the anatomical location)?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Further detailing the directionality of the location of the --TERM (e.g., ANTERIOR, LOWER, PROXIMAL). This may be preprinted or collected. Sponsors may collect the data using a subset list of CT on the CRF.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Qualifier for anatomical location, further detailing the distribution (i.e., arrangement of, apportioning of).
Question Text: What [is/was] the portion or totality of the (anatomical location) involved?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Further detailing the portion or totality of the location of the --TERM. This may be preprinted or collected.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The party accountable for the transferable object (e.g., device, specimen) as a result of the activity performed in the associated --TERM variable.
Question Text: Who [is/was] the accountable party?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Sponsor-defined controlled terminology. The party could be an individual (e.g., subject), an organization (e.g., sponsor), or a location that is a proxy for an individual or organization (e.g., site). This is usually a somewhat general term that is further identified in the -- PRTYID variable (e.g., SITE, SPONSOR, SUBJECT).

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Identification of the specific party accountable for the transferable object (e.g., device, specimen) after the action in -- TERM is taken.
Question Text: What [is/was] the accountable party identifier?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Used in conjunction with --PARTY. This identifier is defined by the sponsor.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The severity or intensity of the event.
Question Text: What [is/was] the severity (of the event topic)?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: In the AE domain, AESEV uses CDISC Controlled Terminology (AESEV).

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A description of the action taken with study treatment as a result of the event.
Question Text: What action was taken with study treatment?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: How to handle multiple actions taken is a sponsor-specific decision. This variable is not to be used for actions taken with devices. See the SDTMIG-MD for information on reporting multiple actions and actions with multiple devices.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication whether this event met the definition of serious.
Question Text: [Is/Was] [the event topic/it] serious?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication the serious event occurred with an overdose.
Question Text: Did the adverse event occur with an overdose?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Although deprecated, this variable is included for illustrative purposes if the sponsor is continuing to collect legacy data. If the details regarding an SAE are collected in the clinical database, then it is recommended that a separate Yes/No variable be defined for each SAE type.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication whether additional categories for seriousness apply.
Question Text: [Is/Was] the adverse event a medically important event not covered by other "serious" criteria?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: If the details regarding an SAE are collected in the clinical database, then it is recommended that a separate Yes/No variable be defined for each SAE type.

Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication whether the SAE was associated with a persistent or significant disability or incapacity.
Question Text: Did the adverse event result in disability or permanent damage?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication whether the SAE resulted in death.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication whether the SAE resulted in an initial or prolonged hospitalization.

Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
