DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication whether the SAE was life threatening.
Question Text: [Is/Was] the adverse event life threatening?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: If the details regarding an SAE are collected in the clinical database, then it is recommended that a separate Yes/No variable be defined for each SAE type.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A description of other action(s) taken as a result of the event that are unrelated to dose adjustments of the study treatment.
Question Text: What other action(s) [were/was] taken?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A description of the action taken, with respect to a device used in a study, which may or may not be the device under study, as a result of the event.
Question Text: What action was taken with the device used in the study?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Sponsor-defined controlled terminology. This variable is intended to be used like --ACN, but refers to device rather than study treatment. See the SDTMIG-MD for information on reporting multiple actions and actions with multiple devices.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The investigator's opinion as to the relationship of the event to the study treatment.
Question Text: [Is/Was] this event related to study treatment?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Sponsor-defined controlled terminology. ICH E2A and E2B examples include NOT RELATED, UNLIKELY RELATED, POSSIBLY RELATED, RELATED.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A description of the investigator's opinion as to whether the event may have been due to a treatment other than study treatment.
Question Text: What [is/was] the relationship to non-study treatment?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: May be reported as free text. Example: "MORE LIKELY RELATED TO ASPIRIN USE".

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A description of the pattern of the event over time.
Question Text: What [is/was] the pattern of the event over time?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Sponsor-defined controlled terminology

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A description of the outcome of an event.
Question Text: What [is/was] the outcome (of the event/event topic)?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: CDISC Controlled Terminology (OUT) is used in the AE domain.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication whether a concomitant or additional was treatment given because of the occurrence of the event.
Question Text: Was a concomitant or additional treatment given (due to this [event])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A description of toxicity quantified by --TOXGR (e.g., NCI CTCAE Short Name).
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column. The sponsor is expected to provide the toxicity scale name and version used to map the terms utilizing the Define-XML external codelist attributes.
Implementation Notes: If used, --TOX would be populated with the decoded value from --TOXGR. For example, if --TERM is HYPERTENSION, and --TOXGR is "1", --TOX is populated with "Prehypertension (systolic BP 120 - 139 mm Hg or diastolic BP 80 - 89 mm Hg)".

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The toxicity grade, using a standard toxicity scale (e.g., NCI CTCAE).
Question Text: What [is/was] the [NCI CTCAE/Name of Scale] grade?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column. The sponsor is expected to provide the toxicity scale name and version used to map the terms utilizing the Define-XML external codelist attributes.
Implementation Notes: See ICH E3 Guidelines for CSR, Section 12.2.4.a CTCAE grade is commonly used in oncology studies although it can also be used elsewhere. Other published toxicity-like scales may be used.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication whether the event is ongoing as of a given timepoint when no end date is provided.
Question Text: [Is/Was] the [--TERM/event topic] ongoing (as of [the study-specific timepoint or period])?
Prompt: Ongoing (as of the [Study-Specific Timepoint or Period])
Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTM variable. May be used to populate a value into an SDTM relative timing variable such as --ENRF or --ENRTPT. When populating --ENRF, or --ENRTPT, if the value of the CDASH field --ONGO is "Y", a value from the CDISC CT (STENRF) may be used. When --ONGO refers to the study reference period (defined in DM.RFSTDTC to DM.RFENDTC), the SDTM variable --ENRF should be populated. When --ONGO is compared to another time point, the SDTM variables --ENRTPT and --ENTPT should be used. Note: --ENRTPT must refer to the time point anchor described in --ENTPT.
Implementation Notes: The CDASH field --ONGO allows specific question text/prompt about events ending prior to the given timepoint. Select the appropriate text when designing the CRF. This may also included in the CRF title or instructions. Used in conjunction with either a reference timepoint (--ENTPT, --ENRTPT) or in conjunction with the study reference period (described as RFSTDTC to RFENDTC). May also be used as a tick/checkbox. See CDASHIG Section 3.7 for more information.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication whether the event caused the subject to discontinue from the study.
Question Text: Did the [--TERM/event topic] cause the subject to be discontinued from the study?
Mapping Instructions: Does not map directly to an SDTM variable. For SDTM submission datasets, may be used to create a RELREC to link Event and Disposition records. The sponsor could submit "--DIS" in a SUPP-- dataset as a value of SUPP--.QVAL where SUPP--.QNAM is "--DIS" and SUPP--.QLABEL is "Caused Study Discontinuation", if appropriate.
Implementation Notes: May be used to create a RELREC to link the record to a Disposition record (see SDTMIG). The sponsor could submit "--DIS" in a SUPP-- dataset as a value of SUPP--.QVAL where SUPP--.QNAM is "--DIS" and SUPP--.QLABEL is "Caused Study Discontinuation", if appropriate.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication that the disease/symptoms were under control at the time of data collection.
Question Text: [Is/Was] the [--TERM/event topic] under control?
Mapping Instructions: This information could be submitted in a SUPP-- dataset as the value of SUPP--.QVAL where SUPP--.QNAM is "--CTRL" and SUPP--.QLABEL is "Disease or Symptom Under Control". Refer to the current SDTM and SDTMIG for instructions on placement of non-standard variables (NSVs) in SDTM domains.
Implementation Notes: The conditions would typically be defined in the protocol. For example, a sponsor may ask the site to indicate whether a subject's hypertension is under control at the time of enrollment in the study.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A description of the reason for the event.
Question Text: What [is/was] the reason (for the [--TERM/event topic])?
Mapping Instructions: This information could be submitted in a SUPP-- dataset as the value of SUPP--.QVAL where SUPP--.QNAM is "--REAS" and SUPP--.QLABEL is "Reason for the Event". Refer to the current SDTM and SDTMIG for instructions on placement of NSVs in SDTM domains.
Implementation Notes: To ensure data quality, it is recommended that sponsors develop controlled terminology. Reason for healthcare encounters could include LACK OF EFFICACY, ADVERSE EVENT, CHEMOTHERAPY, PHYSICAL THERAPY, INDICATION UNDER STUDY, NOT INDICATION UNDER STUDY.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A free-text comment.
Question Text: [Protocol-specified Targeted Question]?
Prompt: [abbreviated version of the protocol-specified targeted question]
Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTM variable. For the SDTM dataset, the CDASH variable COVAL maps to the SDTM variable COVAL (COVAL2, COVAL3) in the CO domain. Associate the free-text comment in the CO domain with the original record using RDOMAIN, IDVAR, IDVARVAL, COREF.
Implementation Notes: If an additional free-text field is needed to provide a comment about a particular record, use the COVAL(n) field to collect the free text, and associate the free-text comment with the original record using SDTM variables RDOMAIN, IDVAR, IDVARVAL, COREF. See the SDTMIG for more information.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: If the value for --TERM is modified for coding purposes, then the modified text is placed here.
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: This is not a data collection field that will appear on the CRF; sponsors will populate this through the coding process. Rationale for adding coding variables to CDASH is to provide a more complete data management package.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The dictionary-derived text description of the lowest level term.
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column. The sponsor is expected to provide the dictionary name and version used to code the event utilizing the Define-XML external codelist attributes. Sponsors should include an Origin column in the define metadata document to indicate that the data was "ASSIGNED".
Implementation Notes: This field does not typically appear on the CRF. Sponsors will populate this through the coding process (e.g., using MedDRA). Rationale for adding coding variables to CDASH is to provide a more complete data management package.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The dictionary-derived code for the lowest level term.
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column. The sponsor is expected to provide the dictionary name and version used to code the event utilizing the Define-XML external codelist attributes. Sponsors should include an Origin column in the define metadata document to indicate that the data was "ASSIGNED".
Implementation Notes: This field does not typically appear on the CRF. Sponsors will populate this through the coding process (e.g., using MedDRA). Rationale for adding coding variables to CDASH is to provide a more complete data management package.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The dictionary-derived code for the preferred term.
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column. The sponsor is expected to provide the dictionary name and version used to code the event utilizing the Define-XML external codelist attributes. Sponsors should include an Origin in the define metadata to indicate that the data was "ASSIGNED".
Implementation Notes: This field does not typically appear on the CRF. Sponsors will populate this through the coding process (e.g., using MedDRA). Rationale for adding coding variables to CDASH is to provide a more complete data management package.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The dictionary-derived text description of the primary system organ class (SOC).
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column. The sponsor is expected to provide the dictionary name and version used to code the event utilizing the Define-XML external codelist attributes. Sponsors should include an Origin column in the define metadata document to indicate that the data was "ASSIGNED".
Implementation Notes: This field does not typically appear on the CRF. Sponsors will populate this through the coding process (e.g., using MedDRA). Rationale for adding coding variables to CDASH is to provide a more complete data management package.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The dictionary-derived code for the primary system organ class (SOC).
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column. The sponsor is expected to provide the dictionary name and version used to code the event utilizing the Define-XML external codelist attributes. Sponsors should include an Origin column in the define metadata document to indicate that the data was "ASSIGNED".
Implementation Notes: This field does not typically appear on the CRF. Sponsors will populate this through the coding process (e.g., using MedDRA). Rationale for adding coding variables to CDASH is to provide a more complete data management package.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The dictionary-derived text description of the high level term for the primary system organ class (SOC).
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column. The sponsor is expected to provide the dictionary name and version used to code the event utilizing the Define-XML external codelist attributes. Sponsors should include an Origin column in the define metadata document to indicate that the data was "ASSIGNED".
Implementation Notes: This field does not typically appear on the CRF. Sponsors will populate this through the coding process (e.g., using MedDRA). Rationale for adding coding variables to CDASH is to provide a more complete data management package.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The dictionary-derived code for the high level term for the primary system organ class (SOC).
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column. The sponsor is expected to provide the dictionary name and version used to code the event utilizing the Define-XML external codelist attributes. Sponsors should include an Origin column in the define metadata document to indicate that the data was "ASSIGNED".
Implementation Notes: This field does not typically appear on the CRF. Sponsors will populate this through the coding process (e.g., using MedDRA). Rationale for adding coding variables to CDASH is to provide a more complete data management package.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The dictionary-derived text description of the high level group term for the primary system organ class (SOC).
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column. The sponsor is expected to provide the dictionary name and version used to code the event utilizing the Define-XML external codelist attributes. Sponsors should include an Origin column in the define metadata document to indicate that the data was "ASSIGNED".
Implementation Notes: This field does not typically appear on the CRF. Sponsors will populate this through the coding process (e.g., using MedDRA). Rationale for adding coding variables to CDASH is to provide a more complete data management package.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The dictionary-derived code for the high level group term for the primary system organ class (SOC).
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column. The sponsor is expected to provide the dictionary name and version used to code the event utilizing the Define-XML external codelist attributes. Sponsors should include an Origin column in the define metadata document to indicate that the data was "ASSIGNED".
Implementation Notes: This field does not typically appear on the CRF. Sponsors will populate this through the coding process (e.g., using MedDRA). Rationale for adding coding variables to CDASH is to provide a more complete data management package.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The way in which the event contact was conducted.
Question Text: What was the contact mode?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Terms like IN PERSON", "TELEPHONE", "IVRS" may be used.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication whether the event was changed due to an epidemic or pandemic.
Question Text: Was the [event topic] changed due to an epidemic or pandemic?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Not the same as whether a medical condition was caused by an epidemic or pandemic.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A judgement as to the likelihood that the device caused the event.
Question Text: [Is/Was] this event related to a device?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: The device may or may not be the device under study. Controlled terminology from EC Directive MEDDEV 2.7/3b is required in EU but not US.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication whether or not any data was collected for the finding topic.
Question Text: Has the subject had any [Findings topic(s)] ([study specific time frame])?; [Was/Were/Is] (there) [a/any] [Findings topic(s)] (reported/available) ([study specific time frame])?; Were all eligibility criteria met?
Mapping Instructions: Does not map to an SDTM variable. The SDTM annotated CRF indicates that this field is NOT SUBMITTED.
Implementation Notes: This is a field that can be used in any CRF to indicate whether or not there are data to record. Used primarily as a data-cleaning field or to dynamically drive EDC form functionality, this provides verification that all other fields on the CRF were deliberately left blank. For example, used in the DD domain to indicate no information on any death details are being provided, or used in the IE domain to indicate whether the subject met all the eligibility requirements for this study at the time the subject was enrolled. \n --PERF should be used to capture a response about whether planned measurements, tests, or observations were done.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication of whether or not a planned measurement, series of measurements, test, observation or specimen was performed or collected.
Question Text: [Were (any)/Was (the)] [--TEST/topic] ([measurement(s)/test(s)/examination(s)/question(s)/assessment(s)/specimen(s)/sample(s)]) [performed/collected]?
Mapping Instructions: This field does not map directly to an SDTM variable. May be used to populate a value into the SDTM variable --STAT. If the CDASH variable --PERF="N", the value of the STDM variable --STAT is "NOT DONE". If --PERF= "Y", --STAT is null. A combination of SDTM variables (e.g., --CAT and --SCAT, --TPT ) is used to indicate that multiple tests were not done. In this situation, the SDTM variable --TESTCD would be populated with --ALL and an appropriate test name (--TEST) provided. See SDTMIG for additional information.
Implementation Notes: This field is used to capture a response to whether or not a planned measurement, test, or observation was performed. A negative response can be collected as "N" and mapped to the --STAT variable in SDTM as "NOT DONE".

CDASH Variable Label: Short Name of Measurement, Test, or Exam
DRAFT CDASH Definition: Short character value code for the test being performed.
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column. The SDTM variable --TESTCD may be determined from the value collected in --TEST. The SDTMIG variables --TESTCD and --TEST are required in SDTM.
Implementation Notes: May be used as a column heading when converting from a vertical dataset format to a horizontal dataset format. --TESTCD is most useful as a variable name or variable naming fragment (e.g., [--TESTCD_--ORRES]) for the clinical database or EDC system for the field in which the result is collected for that test. The short value can be up to 8 characters. When individual codelist names are used for each domain, the CDASH model does not include any Codelist name.

CDASH Variable Label: Name of Measurement, Test, or Exam
DRAFT CDASH Definition: Descriptive name for the test being performed. Examples: Platelet, Systolic Blood Pressure, Summary (Min) RR Duration, Eye Examination.
Question Text: What [is/was] the name (of the [measurement/test/examination/question/assessment])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column. The SDTM variable --TESTCD may be determined from the value collected in --TEST. The SDTMIG variables --TESTCD and --TEST are required in SDTM.
Implementation Notes: The test name will usually be preprinted on the CRF, and not solicited as a question. If the form is laid out as a grid, then the column can be headed Test or Test Name. --TEST is most useful as the PROMPT on the field in which the RESULT for that test is collected. When individual codelist names are used for each domain, the CDASH model does not include any Codelist name.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A further description of --TESTCD and --TEST.
Question Text: What [is/was] the [measurement/test/examination/question/assessment] detail name?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: It is recommended that the test detail name be preprinted on the CRF. If the form is laid out as a grid, then the column can be headed Test or Test Name. When individual codelist names are used for each domain, the CDASH model does not include any Codelist name.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A grouping of topic-variable values based on user-defined characteristics.
Question Text: What [is/was] the [type/category/name] (of the [measurement/test/examination/question/assessment/specimen/sample])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Sponsor-defined controlled terminology. This would most commonly be a heading on the CRF or screen, not a question to which the site would provide an answer. --SCAT can only be used if there is a --CAT. See examples in the CDASHIG metadata table and in the SDTMIG. If a question is asked, the response would typically be a sponsor-defined codelist. If the form is laid out as a grid, then Category (for example) could be included as the column header. Examples are provided in the CDASHIG metadata table or in the SDTMIG. Note: CDISC Controlled Terminology (IECAT) is used for the IE domain.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A sub-division of the --CAT values based on user-defined characteristics.
Question Text: What [is/was] the [type/subcategory/name] (of the [measurement/test/examination/question/assessment/specimen/sample])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Sponsor-defined controlled terminology. This would most commonly be a heading on the CRF or screen, not a question to which the site would provide an answer. --SCAT can only be used if there is a --CAT. Examples are provided in the CDASHIG metadata table and in the SDTMIG. If a question is asked, the response would typically be a sponsor-defined codelist. If the form is laid out as a grid, then Subcategory (for example) could be included in the column header. --SCAT can only be used if there is a --CAT. Examples are provided in the CDASHIG metadata table and in the SDTMIG.

CDASH Variable Label: Result or Finding in Original Units
DRAFT CDASH Definition: Result of the measurement or finding, as originally received or collected.
Question Text: What [is/was] the [result/amount/(subject's) characteristic] (of the [measurement/test/examination/question/assessment])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: In most cases, the Question Text and Item Prompt for --ORRES are specific to the --TEST. The value of --TEST is most useful as the PROMPT on the field in which the RESULT for that test is collected. If the form is laid out as a grid, then Result (for example) could be included in the column header. On CRFs used for drug accountability, the prompt and question text can reflect that the "amount" of study drug dispensed/returned is collected rather than a result.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The unit of the result, as originally received or collected.
Question Text: What [is/was] the unit (of the [measurement/test/examination/question/assessment])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: The Question Text and Item Prompt for --ORRESU may be specific to the --TEST. Should be preprinted on the CRF with the associated test when possible, rather than collected in a free-text field. Note: CDISC Controlled Terminology is used for the SDTMIG PP and VS domains (i.e., PKUNIT, VSRESU).

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The unit of the result if it were collected as a non-standard unit.
Question Text: What [is/was] the unit (of the [measurement/test/examination/question/assessment])?
Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTM variable. The collected non-standard unit(s) may be submitted in a SUPP--dataset as the value of SUPP--.QVAL when SUPP--.QNAM = --CRESU and SUPP.QLABEL= "Collected Non-Standard Unit". Refer to the current SDTM and SDTMIG for instructions on placement of non-standard variables (NSVs) in SDTM domains.
Implementation Notes: The collected non-standard unit(s) should be reported as an equivalent standard unit in --ORRESU.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Text description of any findings.
Question Text: What [is/was] the (description) of the [(abnormality/observed finding/Sponsor-defined)]?
Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTM variable. For the SDTM submission dataset, if the CDASH field -- RES = "NORMAL/ABSENT", populate the SDTM variables --ORRES and --STRESC with the value of the CDASH field --RES. If the value of the CDASH field --RES is "ABNORMAL/PRESENT", populate the SDTM variable --ORRES with the CDASH field --DESC. If the reported findings in --DESC are coded using a dictionary, then the SDTM variable --STRESC is populated with the dictionary preferred term and --MODIFY is populated with the modified text used for coding. If the reported findings in --DESC are not coded, then the SDTM variable --STRESC is populated with the CDASH --DESC field. The SDTM variable --NRIND may be populated with "NORMAL" or "ABNORMAL" if appropriate.
Implementation Notes: --RES and --DESC are used when a question is asked to collect the finding result, with a follow-up question for a description of the finding. See CDASHIG Section 8.3.1, General CDASH Assumptions for Findings Domains.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The result of the measurement or finding, as originally received or collected.
Question Text: What [is/was] the [result/amount] (of the [measurement/test/examination/question/assessment ] )?; [Is/Was] the result [normal/abnormal/absent/present/ sponsored defined response]?
Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTM variable. See CDASH variables --DESC or --RESOTH for mapping information.
Implementation Notes: The CDASH field --RES is used when the collected results are not mapped directly to the SDTM variable --ORRES. --RES is typically used in conjunction with --DESC, or --RESOTH.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A free-text result providing further information about the original received or collected result.
Question Text: If other is selected, [explain/specify/provide more detail]?
Mapping Instructions: When using this CDASH field, the "OTHER" value collected in the CDASH field --RES is mapped to the SDTM variable --STRESC and the value in the CDASH field --RESOTH is mapped to the SDTM variable --ORRES.
Implementation Notes: --RES and ---RESOTH are used when a question is asked that allows the selection of a prespecified finding, with a follow-up question to ask about the prespecified response "OTHER". See CDASHIG Section 8.3.1, General Assumptions for Findings Domains.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A categorization of the result of a finding.
Question Text: What [is/was] the result category (of the [measurement/test/examination/question/assessment])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Sponsor-defined controlled terminology. Example: MALIGNANT or BENIGN for tumor findings. RESISTANCE VARIANT for genetic variation. Note: CDISC Controlled Terminology (MSRESCAT) is used for the MS domain.

CDASH Variable Label: Normal Range Lower Limit- Original Units
DRAFT CDASH Definition: The lower end of normal range or reference range for continuous results stored in --ORRES.
Question Text: What [is/was] the lower limit of the reference range (for the [measurement/test/examination/question/assessment])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: --ORNRLO should be populated only for continuous findings. The SDTM variable --STNRC should be populated only for non-continuous results. These data may be obtained from the lab or the electronic equipment. These data could be derived from a site- or lab-specific set of normal ranges stored in a look-up table.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The upper end of normal range or reference range for continuous results stored in --ORRES.
Question Text: What [is/was] the upper limit of the reference range (for the [measurement/test/examination/question/assessment])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: --ORNRHI should be populated only for continuous findings. The SDTM variable --STNRC should be populated only for non-continuous results. These data may be obtained from the lab or the electronic equipment. These data could be derived from a site- or lab-specific set of normal ranges stored in a look-up table.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The normal reference ranges that are expressed as characters ("Negative to Trace") or ordinal (-1 to 1).
Question Text: What [is/was] the normal reference range (for this [measurement/test/examination/question/assessment])?
Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTM variable. For the SDTM submission dataset, maps to --STNRC.
Implementation Notes: Should be populated for normal ranges that are reported as character in ordinal scale or if categorical ranges were supplied. These data may be obtained from the lab or the electronic equipment. These data could be derived from a site- or lab-specific set of normal ranges stored in a look-up table.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication or description of how the value compares to the normal range or reference range.
Question Text: How [did/do] the reported values compare within the [reference/normal/expected] range?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Reference ranges may be defined by --ORNRLO, --ORNRHI, --STNRC or other objective criteria. Reference Range Indicator (e.g., Y, N; HIGH, LOW; NORMAL; ABNORMAL) may be included if not derived or determined programmatically after data collection. Should not be used to indicate clinical significance. Should not be used to indicate clinical significance.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The variable used to indicate that data are not available, by having the site record the value as "Not Done".
Question Text: Was the [--TEST ] not [completed/answered/done/assessed/evaluated]?; Indicate if (the [--TEST] was) not [answered/assessed/done/evaluated/performed].
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column. If collected, the Origin (a column in the Define-XML) ="CRF", if populated from other sources such as a free-text or sponsor-defined listing for --REASND, the Origin ="DERIVED".
Implementation Notes: Used only when the response value is collected as NOT DONE or NULL in lieu of or in addition to the CDASH --PERF field. Typically a check box which indicates the test was NOT DONE. This field can be useful when multiple questions are asked to confirm that a blank result field is intentionally left blank.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An explanation for why the data are not available.
Question Text: What [is/was] the reason that the [Findings topic/data/information/sponsor-defined phrase] was not [collected/answered/done/assessed/evaluated]?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Sponsor-defined controlled terminology may be used. The reason data are not available may be chosen from a sponsor-defined codelist (e.g., broken equipment, subject refused) or entered as free text. When --REASND is used, --STAT should also be populated in the SDTM-based dataset.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Name or identifier of the vendor (e.g., laboratory) providing the test results.
Question Text: What was the name of the [vendor] used?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Recommended to collect on the CRF if vendor name was not collected at the site/study level or if multiple vendors are used by a site

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The Logical Observation Identifiers Names and Codes (LOINC) code for the topic variable (e.g., a lab test).
Question Text: What [is/was] the LOINC code?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The type of specimen used for a measurement.
Question Text: What [is/was] the specimen (material) type?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The specific anatomical or biological region of a tissue, organ specimen, or the region from which the specimen is obtained, as defined in the protocol (e.g, a section or part of what is described in the --SPEC variable).
Question Text: What [is/was] the anatomical or biological region (of the [organ specimen/tissue])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: The SDTM variable --ANTREG is defined as a variable qualifier of the SDTM variable --SPEC.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The condition of the specimen.
Question Text: What [is/was] the condition of the specimen?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Standardized text describing the condition of the sample (e.g., HEMOLYZED, LIPEMIC)

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication about the usability of the specimen for obtaining the test result.
Question Text: What is/was the usability (of this specimen)?; [Is/Was] the specimen usable?
Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTM variable. For the SDTM dataset, if the CDASH variable --CSPUFL="Y", the value of the STDM variable --CSPUFL is "Y". If CSPULF="N", --CSPULF is null.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The position of the subject during a measurement or examination.
Question Text: In what position was the subject during the [measurement/test/examination/question/assessment/specimen collection/sample collection]?; What was the position of the subject (during the [measurement/test/examination/question/assessment/specimen collection/sample collection])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Note: CDISC Controlled Terminology (VSPOS) is used in the VS domain.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The anatomical location of the subject relevant to the collection of the measurement.
Question Text: What [is/was] the anatomical location (of the [measurement/test/examination/question/assessment])?; What [is/was] the anatomical location where the [measurement/specimen/question/assessment] was [taken/collected]?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: This may be preprinted or collected. --LOC is used only to specify the anatomical location. --LAT, --DIR, --PORTOT are used to further describe the anatomical location.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Qualifier for anatomical location, further detailing the side of the body.
Question Text: What [is/was] the side (of the anatomical location of the [measurement/test/examination/question/assessment])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Further detailing the laterality of the location of the --TEST. This may be preprinted or collected.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Qualifier further detailing the position of the anatomical location, relative to the center of the body, organ, or specimen.
Question Text: What [is/was] the directionality (of the anatomical location of the [measurement/test/examination/question/assessment])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Further detailing the directionality of the location of the --TEST (e.g., ANTERIOR, LOWER, PROXIMAL). This may be preprinted or collected. Sponsors may collect the data using a subset list of CT on the CRF.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A detailed description of the location of the identified finding.
Question Text: What [were/are] additional details on the exact location of the [finding] so that it can be distinguished from other [findings] in the same anatomical location?
Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTM variable. This information could be submitted in a SUPP-- dataset as the value of SUPP--.QVAL where SUPP--.QNAM="--LOCDTL" and SUPP--.QLABEL="Location Detail".
Implementation Notes: Use if --LOC and --LAT and/or --DIR values cannot provide uniqueness from other identified findings. --LOCDTL is not meant to replace --LOC, --LAT, and/or --DIR, or to serve as the free-text description field for --LOC (e.g., Location, Other).

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Qualifier for anatomical location further detailing the distribution (i.e., arrangement of, apportioning of).
Question Text: What [is/was] the portion or totality (of the anatomical location of the [measurement/test/examination/question/assessment])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Further detailing the portion or totality of the location of the --TEST. This may be pre-printed or collected.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The method of the test or examination.
Question Text: What was the method (used for the [measurement/test/examination/question/assessment])?; What was the method (used to [measure/test/examine/question/assess/evaluate/identify] the [finding])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Note: CDISC Controlled Terminology (EGMETHOD) is used in the EG domain.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The lead or leads identified to capture the measurement for a test from an instrument.
Question Text: What [is/was] the lead (used to measure [measurement/test/examination/question/assessment])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Note: CDISC Controlled Terminology (EGLEAD) is used in the EG domain.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The consciousness state of the subject at the time of measurement.
Question Text: What [is/was] the consciousness state of the subject (at the time of the [measurement/test/examination/question/assessment])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication as to whether the subject had abstained from food/water for the specified amount of time.
Question Text: [Is/Was] the subject fasting (prior to the [test being performed/sample being collected])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The role of the person providing the evaluation.
Question Text: Who provided the (sponsor-defined phrase) information?; Who was the evaluator?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Used only for results that are subjective (e.g., assigned by a person or a group). May be a preprinted or collected. Sponsors may collect the data using a subset list of CT on the CRF. Not used in QS, FT, and clinical classifications use case of RS.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An identifier used to distinguish multiple evaluators with the same role, recorded in --EVAL.
Question Text: What [is/was] the identifier of the [evaluator name/reporter name] (providing the-sponsor-defined phrase- information)?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Not used in QS, FT, and clinical classifications use case of RS.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication that the evaluation is considered, by an independent assessor, to be the accepted or final evaluation.
Question Text: [Is/Was] this record considered to be the [accepted/final] evaluation?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Used where more than 1 assessor provides an evaluation of a result or response. Typically a checkbox with the value of "Y" or "NULL", which indicates the evaluation was accepted.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The description of toxicity quantified by --TOXGR (e.g., NCI CTCAE Short Name).
Question Text: What [is/was] the description of the [NCI CTCAE/scale name] toxicity?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Sponsors may choose not to collect --TOX. If collected, sponsors should specify which scale and version is used in the Sponsor Comments column of the Define-XML document.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The toxicity grade using a standard toxicity scale (e.g., NCI CTCAE).
Question Text: What [is/was] the [NCI CTCAE Toxicity/scale name] grade?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Sponsors may choose not to collect --TOXGR. If collected, sponsors should specify which scale and version is used in the Sponsor Comments column of the Define- XML document. Note: CDISC Controlled Terminology (TOXGRV3 or TOXGRV4) may be used for this variable.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The severity or intensity of a particular finding.
Question Text: What [is/was] the severity (of the finding)?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication whether the test results were clinically significant.
Question Text: [Is/Was] the ([measurement/test/examination/question/assessment]) result clinically significant?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication of the relationship of a particular finding to the death of a subject.
Question Text: [Is/Was] this findings related to the death of the subject?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The collected lower limit of quantitation for an assay, represented in text format or as a range, such as less than a specified numeric value.
Question Text: What [is/was] the lower limit of quantification (for the [measurement/test/examination])?
Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTM variable. For the SDTM dataset, the CDASH variable, --CLLOQ maps to the SDTM variable --LLOQ. The units will be those used for --STRESU.
Implementation Notes: These data may be obtained directly from the lab or the electronic equipment and not collected on the CRF. The units are those used for the SDTM variable --STRESU. This is not the lower limit of normal of the reference range for the test. The SDTM variable --LLOQ must be populated as a numeric.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The collected upper limit of quantitation for an assay, represented in text format or as a range, such as greater than a specified numeric value.
Question Text: What [is/was] the upper limit of quantification (for the [measurement/test/examination])?
Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTM variable. For the SDTM dataset, the CDASH variable, --CULOQ maps to the SDTM variable --ULOQ. The units will be those used for --STRESU.
Implementation Notes: These data may be obtained directly from the lab or the electronic equipment and not collected on the CRF. The units are those used for the SDTM variable --STRESU. This is not the upper limit of normal of the reference range for the test. The SDTM variable --ULOQ must be populated as a numeric.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication whether the testing conditions defined in the protocol were met (e.g., low-fat diet).
Question Text: [Are/Were] the protocol-defined testing conditions met?
Mapping Instructions: This information could be submitted in a SUPP--dataset as the value of SUPP--.QVAL when SUPP--.QNAM = --COND and SUPP.QLABEL="Test Condition Met". Refer to the current SDTM and SDTMIG for instructions on placement of NSVs in SDTM domains.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The instance number of a test that is repeated within a given timeframe for the same test. The level of granularity can vary (e.g., within a time point or within a visit).
Question Text: What was the repetition number within (the) [time point/visit/timeframe] (for this [measurement/test/examination/question/assessment])?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: The repetition number of the test/measurement within the time point may be pre-printed on the CRF (e.g., multiple measurements of blood pressure, multiple analyses of a sample).

CDASH Variable Label: Same as Previous Sample Collection Date
DRAFT CDASH Definition: A flag indicating that the date (or start date) is the same as the previous specimen collection date (or start date).
Question Text: [Is/Was] this specimen/sample collected on the same date as the (last/previous specimen/sample) (collected/collection ended)?
Mapping Instructions: Does not map to an SDTM variable. The SDTM annotated CRF indicates that this field is NOT SUBMITTED.
Implementation Notes: When a series of specimens are recorded on a single CRF, this field is tied to the collection date to allow for the flag to be used as a surrogate for the date field. Its selection means that the date of this specimen is the same as the date of the last specimen collected (in the series). Usually a checkbox or tick box on the CRF. This would typically be used in the PC domain.

CDASH Variable Label: Same as Current Sample Collection Start Date
DRAFT CDASH Definition: A flag indicating that the specimen/sample collection ended on the same date the current/previous specimen collection started.
Question Text: [Is/Was] this specimen/sample collection ended on the same day as the current specimen's/sample's start date?
Prompt: Same as Current (Specimen's/Sample Collection) Start Date
Mapping Instructions: Does not map to an SDTM variable. The SDTM annotated CRF indicates that this field is NOT SUBMITTED.
Implementation Notes: When a series of interval sample/specimen collections are recorded on a single CRF, this field is tied to the start of the current collection to allow for the flag to be used as a surrogate for the date field. Its selection means that the end date of this specimen/sample collection is the same as the start date of the current specimen/sample collected. Usually a checkbox or tick box on the CRF. This would typically be used in the PC domain.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A free-text comment.
Question Text: [Protocol-specified Targeted Question]?
Prompt: [abbreviated version of the protocol-specified targeted question]
Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTM variable. For the SDTM dataset, the CDASH variable --COVAL maps to the SDTM variable COVAL (COVAL2, COVAL3) in the CO domain. Associate the free-text comment in the CO domain with the original record using RDOMAIN, IDVAR, IDVARVAL, COREF.
Implementation Notes: If an additional free-text field is needed to provide a comment about a particular record, use the COVAL(n) field to collect the free text, and associate the free-text comment with the original record using SDTM variables RDOMAIN, IDVAR, IDVARVAL, COREF. See the SDTMIG for additional information.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: If the value for --ORRES is modified for coding purposes, then the modified text is placed here.
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: This is not a data collection field that will appear on the CRF; sponsors will populate this through the coding process. The rationale for adding coding variables to CDASH is to provide a more complete data management package.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Body system or organ class that is involved for a finding, from the standard hierarchy for dictionary-coded results.
Question Text: What is/was the [body system/organ system]?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: --BODSYS should be assigned using a coding system. If included on the CRF, it is pre-populated and must be paired by the sponsor with specific prespecified verbatim terms. If not included on the CRF, --BODSYS is assigned through the coding process.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The way in which the event contact was conducted.
Question Text: What was the contact mode?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Terms like "IN PERSON", "TELEPHONE", "IVRS" may be used

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication whether the event was changed due to an epidemic or pandemic.
Question Text: Was the [event topic] changed due to an edidemic or pandemic?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Not the same as whether a medical condition was caused by an epidemic or pandemic.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The sensitivity of the test methodology with respect to observation, detection, or quantification.
Question Text: What is the [test method] sensitivity?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: The sensitivity of the test may be collect with terms luch as "LOW SENSITIVITY", "HIGH SENSITIVITY", "ULTRA-HIGH SENSITIVITY".

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The explanation for why a test, measurement, or assessment is executed.
Question Text: What is the reason the [test] was performed?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A description of the distribution pattern of a finding within the examined area.
Question Text: What is the [pattern/distribution] of the [findings/results] within the examined area?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Terms such as "FOCAL", "MULTIFOCAL", "DIFFUSE" may be used to describe the distribution pattern.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The classification of the kind of result (i.e., property type) originally reported for the test.
Question Text: What is the [classification/type] of the [test/test result]?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Terms such as "SUBSTANCE CONCENTRATION", "MASS RATE", "ARBITRARY CONCENTRATION" may be used.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The classification of the scale of the original result value.
Question Text: What is the [scale classification/type] used for the [test/test result]?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Terms such as "ORDINAL", "NOMINAL", "QUANTITATIVE", "NARRATIVE" may be used.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The planned condition imposed by the assay system on the specimen at the time the test is performed.
Question Text: What is the (planned) [condition] on the specimen?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: This may be terms relating to stimulating or activating agents, assay temperature, incubation time (e.g., "STIMULATED", "NON-STIMULATED", "25 C", "37 C"). Used only in the CP, IS, and LB domains.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The textual description of the agent used to impose a test condition identified in --TSTCND.
Question Text: What is the [test condition agent]?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: The agent would typically be selected from a list of terms, provided by an external vendor, or prespecified in the protocol. Used only in the CP, IS, and LB domains.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The textual description of the agent that's binding to the entity in the --TEST variable.
Question Text: What is the [binding agent] used for the [--test]?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: This typically would prespecified in the protocol and not directly collected or provided by a by an external vendor. The binding agent may be, but is not limited to, a test article, a portion of the test article, a related compound, an endogenous molecule, an allergen, or an infectious agent. Used only in CP, IS, and LB domains.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The textual description of the high-level purpose of the test at the operational level.
Question Text: What is the [operational objective/reason] of the [test]?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Terrms such as "SCREEN", "CONFIRM", "QUANTIFY" may be used.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indicatation of the type of a collected summary result.
Question Text: What type of [(summary)result for the test] is collected?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: This is used for summary results collected on a CRF or provided by an external vendor (e.g., central lab). If the summary result is derived by the sponsor using individual source data records, this is not used. This is usually prespecified by the sponsor.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The characterization of the duration of a biological process resulting in a particular finding.
Question Text: What is the [chronicity of finding of the biological process]?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Terms such as ACUTE", "CHRONIC", "SUBACUTE" may be used.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A sequence of characters used to uniquely identify a particular run of a test on a particular batch of samples.
Question Text: What is the run-id for the [sample/batch/replicate]?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Describes the event or intervention whose property is being measured in --TESTCD/--TEST.
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: The --OBJ will usually be preprinted or hidden, and not solicited as an actual question. These FA domains are usually created by each sponsor. CDASH has used this variable in the SR domain.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An identifier for the entity with the overall regulatory responsibility for the protocol.
Question Text: What is the sponsor identifier?
Mapping Instructions: For the SDTM dataset, the value in the CDASH field SPONSOR maps to the SDTM variable TSVAL. The SDTM variable TSPARMCD is populated with "SPONSOR" and the SDTM variable TSPARM is populated with "Clinical Study Sponsor".
Implementation Notes: In some cases, the combination of Sponsor ID with Study ID and Site ID might be needed by external parties (e.g., CRO or for a multisponsor study) to uniquely identify sites belonging to the study for the given sponsor.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A 2-character abbreviation for the domain most relevant to the observation.
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: This field can be derived into the database or created during SDTM dataset creation before submission. The domain abbreviation is also used as a prefix for variables to ensure uniqueness when datasets are merged.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A unique identifier for a study.
Question Text: What [is/was] the study identifier?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Although this field is not typically captured on a CRF, it should be displayed clearly on the CRF and/or the EDC system. This field can be included into the database or populated during SDTM-based dataset creation before submission.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A unique identifier for a site within a study.
Question Text: What [is/was] the site identifier?
Mapping Instructions: For the SDTM dataset, the value in the CDASH field SITEID maps to the SDTM variable DM.SITEID.
Implementation Notes: Paper: This is typically preprinted in the header of each CRF page for single site studies. For studies with multiple sites, this field may be left blank so that the number can be recorded by the site, or it may be preprinted for the CRFs that are shipped to each site. \n EDC: This should be prepopulated.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An identifier to describe the investigator for the study.
Question Text: What [is/was] the investigator identifier?
Mapping Instructions: For the SDTM dataset, the value in the CDASH field INVID maps to the SDTM variable DM.INVID.
Implementation Notes: May be used in addition to the SITEID. Not needed if SITEID is equivalent to INVID.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A unique subject identifier within a site and a study.
Question Text: What [is/was] the (study) [subject/participant] identifier?
Mapping Instructions: For the SDTM dataset, the value in the CDASH field SUBJID maps to the SDTM variable DM.SUBJID.
Implementation Notes: This CDASH variable is typically collected in all CDASH domains. However, this CDASH variable is populated only in the SDTMIG DM domain. The recording of multiple SUBJID for the same subject is a known SDTM issue. If a subject is screened more than once, there may be more than 1 SUBJID per instance of being screened within the trial. See FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide, Section 4.1.1.2.a

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An identifier used to indicate a focus of study-specific interest on or within a subject or specimen (as described in the protocol), for which a measurement, test, or examination was performed, such as a drug application site (e.g., "Injection site 1", "Biopsy site 1", "Treated site 1") or a more specific focus (e.g., "OD" (right eye) or "Upper left quadrant of the back"). The value in this variable should have inherent semantic meaning.
Question Text: [Protocol specific question]?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: This SDTM variable was defined in SDTM v1.5, but was not included in SDTMIG 3.2. Sponsors should consider the SDTM version being used when creating submission datasets. If using pre-SDTM 1.5, the CDASH-to-SDTM mapping may need to be defined by the sponsor because FOCID was not a valid SDTM variable in these versions.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A sponsor-defined identifier. In CDASH, This is typically used for pre-printed or auto-generated numbers on the CRF, or any other type of identifier that does not already have a defined CDASH identifier field.
Question Text: [Sponsor-defined question]
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column. May be used to create RELREC to link this record with a record in another domain.
Implementation Notes: Because SPID is a sponsor-defined identifier, conformance to Question Text or Item Prompt is not applicable. Typically used as an identifier in a data query to communicate clearly to the site the specific record in question or to reconcile data. May be used to record preprinted number (e.g., line number, record number) on the CRF. This field may be populated by the sponsor's data collection system.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A group identifier used to link together a block of related records within a subject in a domain.
Question Text: What [is/was] the [test/procedure/observation] group identifier?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Used to link together a block of related records in a single domain for a subject. This group identifier may be used to tie together all the tests collected in a Findings domain using a denormalized approach (see CDASHIG Section 8.3.1, General CDASH Assumptions for Findings Domains). This field may be populated by the sponsor's data management system.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An identifier used to link related records across domains.
Question Text: What [is/was] the [test/procedure/observation] identifier?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: This may be a one-to-one or a one-to-many relationship. Example: A single tumor may have multiple measurements/assessments performed at each study visit.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An identifier used to link related records across domains.
Question Text: What [is/was] the [domain/observation] link group ID?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: This will usually be a many-to-one relationship. Example: Multiple tumor measurements/assessments will contribute to a single response-to-therapy determination record.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An internal or external identifier (e.g., lab specimen ID, UUID for an ECG waveform or a medical image).
Question Text: What [is/was] the [test/procedure/domain/observation/specimen/sample/report] [reference identifier/accession number/identifier]?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A numerical identifier for the adverse event that is associated with/related to this intervention/finding/event.
Question Text: What [is/was] the identifier for the adverse event(s) [associated with/related to this intervention/finding/event]?
Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTM variable. For the SDTM submission datasets, may be used to create the RELREC dataset to link this record with a record in the associated Adverse Events domain.
Implementation Notes: Name of the identifying variable is stored in the SDTM variable IDVAR (e.g., --AENO) and the value of the SDTM variable IDVAR is stored in SDTM variable IDVARVAL. Example question text: What was the identifier for the adverse event associated with this concomitant medication?

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A numerical identifier for the medical history event that is associated with/related to this intervention/finding/event.
Question Text: What [is/was] the identifier for the medical history event(s) [associated with/related to this intervention/finding/event]?
Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTM variable. For the SDTM submission datasets, may be used to create the RELREC dataset to link this record with a record in the associated Medical History domain.
Implementation Notes: Name of the identifying variable is stored as a value in the SDTM variable IDVAR (e.g., "--MHNO") and the value of the IDVAR is stored in the STDM variable IDVARVAL. Example question text prompt: What was the identifier for the medical history event associated with this concomitant medication?

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A numerical identifier for the procedure that is associated with/related to this intervention/finding/event.
Question Text: What [is/was] the identifier for the procedure(s) [associated with/related to this intervention/finding/event]?
Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTM variable. For the SDTM submission datasets, may be used to create the RELREC dataset to link this record with a record in the associated Procedures domain.
Implementation Notes: Name of the identifying variable is stored as a value in the SDTM variable IDVAR (e.g., "--PRNO") and the value of the IDVAR is stored in the SDTM variable IDVARVAL. Example question text for an Adverse Event CRF: What was the identifier for the procedure associated with this adverse event?

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A numerical identifier for the clinical event that is associated with/related to this intervention/finding/event.
Question Text: What [is/was] the identifier for the clinical event(s) [associated with/related to this intervention/finding/event]?
Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTM variable. For the SDTM submission datasets, may be used to create the RELREC dataset to link this record with a record in the associated Clinical Events domain.
Implementation Notes: Name of the identifying variable is stored as a value in the SDTM variable IDVAR ("--CENO") and the value of the IDVAR is stored in the SDTM variable IDVARVAL Example question text: What was the identifier for the clinical event associated with this procedure?

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A sponsor-defined identifier for a device.
Question Text: What [is/was] the sponsor identifier for the device?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: SPDEVID is a constructed variable consisting of elements that may not be available at the time of data capture (e.g., device type, manufacturer). Other device identifiers, such as serial number, may instead be used on the CRF. If this is the case, sponsors should use controlled terminology for the SPDEVID elements as variable names where possible. Sponsors should also ensure that, when necessary, those elements required for deriving SPDEVID are captured.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The identifier for a non-host organism; should only be used when the organism is the subject of the test.
Question Text: What [is/was] the Non-host Organism ID?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: This sponsor-defined identifier for a non-host organism should only be used when the organism is the subject of the test.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A sequence number that identifies the beat within an ECG.

Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Used in the EG domain only.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The identifier for a single associated person, a group of associated persons, or a pool of associated persons.
Question Text: What is the [associated person's/group of associated persons/pool of associated persons] identifier?
Prompt: [Associated Person's/Group of Associated Persons/Pool of associated persons] Identifier
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The identifier for the study subject/participant.
Question Text: What [is/was] the related (study) [subject/participant] identifier?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: This may be derived/populated from the study subject's identifier as recorded on the subject Demographics CRF.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The Identifier for a related device.
Question Text: What is the identifier for the related device?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: RDEVID will be populated with the SPDEVID of the related device

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The Identifier for a related subject or pool of subjects.
Question Text: What is the associated person's relationship to the (study) [subject/participant]?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: If RSUBJID is populated, describes the relationship of the associated person(s) identified in APID to the subject or pool identified in RSUBJID. \n If RDEVID is populated, describes the relationship of the associated person(s) identified in APID to the subject or pool identified in RDEVID. \n If RSUBJID and RDEVID are null, SREL describes the relationship of the associated person(s) identified in APID to the study identified in STUDYID.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Clinical encounter number. Numeric version of VISIT can be used for sorting.
Question Text: What is the visit number?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: This is not a data collection field that would appear on the CRF. This field may be populated by the sponsor's data collection system or derived from the variable VISIT. Note: Sponsors may have CDASH visit numbering or visit naming conventions to handle special circumstances (e.g., unscheduled visits). In these cases, the appropriate visit numbers and visit names may need to be populated when the SDTM submission datasets are created.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Protocol-defined description of the clinical encounter. May be used in addition to VISITNUM and/or VISITDY as a text description of the clinical encounter.
Question Text: What [is/was] the visit name?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: The name of the visit is typically preprinted on the CRF, and should match the name of the visit in the protocol. May be used to derive the SDTM variable VISITNUM. Note: Sponsors may have CDASH visit numbering or visit naming conventions to handle special circumstances (e.g., unscheduled visits). In these cases, the appropriate visit numbers and visit names may need to be populated when the SDTM submission datasets are created.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Date the clinical encounter occurred (or started).
Question Text: What [is/was] the date of the visit?
Mapping Instructions: This field does not map directly to an SDTM variable. For the SDTMIG SV dataset, the SDTMIG variable SVSTDTC may be derived by concatenating all collected CDASH VISDAT and VISTIM components and populating the SDTM variable SVSTDTC in ISO 8601 format.
Implementation Notes: This may be recorded in either the header of the CRF or in the body of the CRF. A date/time can be collected once for the whole visit using the Visit Date/Time field and applying that date/time to all of the observations at that visit. The date (--DTC) of a measurement, test, or observation can be determined from the date/time of visit (VISDAT/VISTIM) and then concatenating the CDASH VISDAT/VISTIM components, and populating the STDMIG variable --DTC in ISO 8601 format.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Time the clinical encounter took place (or started).

For SDTM submission datasets, concatenate CDASH VISDAT/VISTIM components, and populate the STDMIG variable --DTC in ISO 8601 format. May be used to populate SVSTDTC, and SVENDTC in the SDTMIG SV domain.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Date the clinical encounter ended.
Question Text: What [is/was] the end date of the visit?
Mapping Instructions: This field does not map directly to an SDTM variable. For the SDTM SV dataset, the SDTMIG variable SVENDTC may be derived by concatenating all collected CDASH VISENDAT and VISENTIM components and populating the SDTM variable SVENDTC in ISO 8601 format.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Name of the trial epoch with which this element of the arm is associated.

Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Collection date of an observation.
Question Text: What [is/was] the date the [event or intervention] [is/was] collected?; What [is/was] the (start) date (of the [Finding])?
Mapping Instructions: This field does not map directly to an SDTM variable. For the SDTM dataset, concatenate all collected CDASH DATE and TIME components and populate the SDTM variable --DTC in ISO 8601 format. Section 4.1.4.1 of the FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guidea indicates that when dates have the role of a timing variable, the matching Study Day variables (--DY, --STDY, or --ENDY, respectively) should be included in the SDTM dataset.
Implementation Notes: This is a generic DATE field that can be implemented in a system that will store partial dates. Use this for date of data collection, visit date, visit start date, point-in-time collection (e.g., vital signs measurements, lab sample collection date), or start date for interval collection of measurements or tests (e.g., start date of a 24-hour urine collection). Section 4.1.4.1 of the FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide v2.2a indicates that when dates have the role of a timing variable, the matching Study Day variables (--DY, --STDY, or --ENDY, respectively) are included in the SDTM dataset.

Question Text: What [is/was] the ([intended/planned/actual]) ([event/intervention/finding]) ([end/discharge/stop]) second (of the observation)?
Mapping Instructions: This field does not map directly to an SDTM variable. For the SDTM dataset, concatenate all collected CDASH End DATE and TIME components and populate the SDTM variable --ENDTC in ISO 8601 format. Section 4.1.4.1 of the FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guidea indicates that when dates have the role of a timing variable, the matching Study Day variables (--DY, --STDY, or --ENDY, respectively) should be included in the SDTM dataset.
Implementation Notes: This is a generic END SECOND (ENSS) field that can be implemented in a system that will not store partial dates. Use this for end time of events (e.g., AE end time) or Interventions (e.g., CM end time), end time of interval dosing (e.g., time of infusion end), end visit time, end time for interval collection of measurements or tests (e.g., and time of 24-hour urine collection).

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Collected duration of an event, intervention, or finding. Used only if collected on the CRF and not derived.
Question Text: What [is/was] the duration of the [event/intervention]?
Mapping Instructions: This field does not map directly to an SDTM variable. For the SDTM dataset, concatenate the collected CDASH duration and the CDASH duration unit components and populate the SDTM variable --DUR in ISO 8601 period format.
Implementation Notes: Use only if collected on the CRF and not derived.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The unit of time associated with the collected duration of an event, intervention, or finding.
Question Text: What [is/was] the duration unit of the [event/intervention]?
Mapping Instructions: This field does not map directly to an SDTM variable. For the SDTM dataset, concatenate the collected CDASH duration and the CDASH duration unit components and populate the SDTM variable --DUR in ISO 8601 period format.
Implementation Notes: Use only if collected on the CRF and not derived.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication whether the event/intervention/finding started or occurred prior to a specified time point.
Question Text: Was the [--TRT/Intervention] [taken/performed/used/administered/consumed/started/occurred] prior to a [specified timepoint]?; Did the [--TERM/event topic/--TRT/Intervention/Finding] [start/occur] prior to [specified timepoint]?
Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTM variable. May be used to populate a value into an SDTM relative timing variable such as --STRF or --STRTPT. When populating --STRF, or --STRTPT, if the value of the CDASH field --PRIOR is "Y" a value from the CDISC CT (STENRF) may be used. When --PRIOR refers to the Study Reference Period (defined in DM.RFSTDTC to DM.RFENDTC) the SDTM variable --STRF should be populated. When --PRIOR is compared to another timepoint, the SDTM variables --STRTPT and --STTPT should be used. Note: --STRTPT must refer to the time point anchor described in --STTPT.
Implementation Notes: The CDASH field --PRIOR allows specific question text and prompt about interventions, events or findings that were prior to a specified timepoint. --PRIOR is used in conjunction with either a reference timepoint (--STTPT, --ENTPT) or the Study Reference Period (described as RFSTDTC to RFENDTC). See CDASHIG Section 3.7 for more information.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Text description of time when a measurement or observation should be taken, as defined in the protocol. This may be represented as an elapsed time relative to a fixed reference point (e.g., time of last dose). See --TPTNUM and --TPTREF.
Question Text: What [is/was] the planned time point [of the/for the] [measurement/observation/collection]?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column. See the SDTMIG for additional information on representing time points. SDTM time point anchors --TPTREF (text description) and --RFTDTC (date/time) may be needed, as well as SDTM variables --TPTNUM, --ELTM.
Implementation Notes: Planned time point names are needed to differentiate multiple sequential assessments. It is recommended that time point names be preprinted on the CRF rather than collected in a free-text field. If the form is laid out as a grid, then words such as Planned Time Point can be included as the column header. In the SDTM submission dataset, time points can be represented using the time point variables --TPT, --TPTNUM,-- ELTM. See the SDTMIG for additional information.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Numeric version of planned time point, used in sorting.
Question Text: What [is/was] the planned time point number [of the/for the] [measurement/observation/collection]?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column. See SDTMIG for additional information on representing time points. SDTM time point anchors --TPTREF (text description) and --RFTDTC (date/time) may be needed, as well as SDTM variables --TPTNUM, --ELTM.
Implementation Notes: Planned time point numbers may be needed to differentiate multiple sequential assessments. If collected, it is recommended that time point numbers be preprinted on the CRF. In the SDTM submission dataset, time points can be represented using the time point variables --TPT, --TPTNUM,-- ELTM. See SDTMIG for additional information.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Description of the fixed reference point referred to by --ELTM, --TPTNUM, and --TPT.
Question Text: What is the description of the fixed reference point?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Planned reference point referred to by time point variables --TPT, TPTNUM, --ELTM. See SDTMIG for additional information. This would most commonly be prespecified on the CRF, and not a question to which the site would provide an answer.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Date for a fixed reference time point defined by --TPTREF.
Question Text: What was the date of the [Reference Time point]?
Mapping Instructions: This field does not map directly to an SDTM variable. For the SDTM dataset, the SDTMIG variable --RFTDTC is derived by concatenating all collected CDASH --RFTDAT, and --RFTTIM components and populating the SDTM variable --RFTDTC in ISO 8601 format.
Implementation Notes: Date of the fix reference time point defined by --TPTREF
