Implementation Notes: Time of the fix reference time point defined by --TPTREF

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The collected or prepopulated text description of an interval associated with an observation such as a finding --TESTCD.
Question Text: [Included as part of a protocol-specified question].
Mapping Instructions: This field does not map directly to an SDTM variable. For the SDTM dataset, convert the collect evaluation interval into an ISO 8601 period format and populate the SDTM variable --EVLINT or if the interval can not be converted to an ISO format, populate the SDTM variable --EVINTX.
Implementation Notes: The CDASH field --CEVINT (which is stored as free text) indicates a period of time during which an observation is being made or about which a question is being asked (e.g., "During the past 6 months what was the subject's average sleep time?" or "Record any significant cardiovascular medical history over the subject's lifetime."). The evaluation interval free text is defaulted in the hidden CDASH field --CEVINT (e.g., PAST 6 MONTHS, LIFETIME). Intervals that can be converted to ISO format (e.g., PAST 6 WEEKS), are mapped to the SDTM variable --EVLINT (-P6W), and free-text interval are mapped to --EVINTX (LIFETIME). See SDTMIG for additional information.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Duration of interval associated with an observation, such as a finding --TESTCD.
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: The CDASH field --EVLINT indicates a period of time during which an observation is being made or about which a question is being asked which is represented in an ISO format. This would be a hidden field on the CRF or screen, not a question to which the site would provide an answer. The evaluation interval is included in the question text and the hidden CDASH field --EVLINT is defaulted using the appropriate ISO format for the interval. Example: "During the past 6 months, what was the subject's average sleep time?" is the question text; the hidden CDASH field --EVLINT would be "-P6M". See the SDTMIG for additional information.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Date of death for any subject who died.
Question Text: What [is/was] the subject's date of death?
Mapping Instructions: This field does not map directly to an SDTM variable. For the SDTM dataset, concatenate all collected CDASH DATE and TIME components and populate the SDTM variable DM.DTHDTC in ISO 8601 format.
Implementation Notes: The CDASH Model defines Death Date as a timing variable. It is not included as a timing variable in the SDTMIG. It may be collected on any CRF deemed appropriate by the sponsor, but should only be collected once. The SDTM variables DTHDTC and DTHFL are mapped to the DM domain during the SDTM submission dataset creation process. Death Date may be mapped to other SDTM domains (e.g., DS), as deemed appropriate by the sponsor. Death Date can be collected as a single field, or as 3 fields (DTHMO, DTHDD and DTHYY).

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The name of a specific instance of a Disease Milestone Type (MIDSTYPE) described in the Trial Disease Milestones dataset.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The start of a planned assessment interval relative to a reference time point, represented in a standardized character format.
Question Text: [A protocol-specified question]
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: The reference time point is in --TPTREF. As this describes planned timing of an assessment, caution should be exercised when using outside of the Findings domains. In an Events or Interventions domain, it may refer to the interval over which --OCCUR is assessed. This would typically be prespecified or specified options provided based on the protocol.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The end of a planned assessment interval relative to a reference time point, represented in a standardized character format.
Question Text: [A protocol-specified question]
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.

In an Events or Interventions domain, it may refer to the interval over which --OCCUR is assessed. This would typically be prespecified or specified options provided based on the protocol.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication that the specimen was collected at a single point in time.

Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Only in Findings class specimen-based domains: BS, CP, GF, IS, LB, MB, MS, MI, PC, PP. The value is "Y" or null.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A planned duration of a finding.
Question Text: What [is/was] the planned duration of the [specimen collection/finding] ?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Only in Findings class specimen-based domains: BS, CP, GF, IS, LB, MB, MS, MI, PC, PP. For a sample-based finding, this could apply to the planned duration of specimen collection.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication whether any other action(s) were taken in response to the adverse event, unrelated to study treatment dose changes, or whether other non-study treatments were given because of this adverse event.
Question Text: Were any other actions taken in response to this adverse event?
Mapping Instructions: Does not map to an SDTM variable. The SDTM annotated CRF indicates that this field is NOT SUBMITTED.
Implementation Notes: The intent/purpose of collecting this field is to help with data cleaning and monitoring. It provides verification that the AEACNOTH field on the CRF was deliberately left blank.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication whether, in the investigator's opinion, the event may have been due to a treatment other than study treatment.
Question Text: [Is/Was] this adverse event due to treatment other than study treatment?
Mapping Instructions: Does not map to an SDTM variable.The SDTM annotated CRF indicates that this field is NOT SUBMITTED.
Implementation Notes: The intent/purpose of collecting this field is to help with data cleaning and monitoring. It provides verification that the CDASH AERELNST field on the CRF was deliberately left blank.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An adverse event of special interest (serious or non-serious) is one of scientific and medical concern specific to the sponsor's product or program, for which ongoing monitoring and rapid communication by the investigator to the sponsor can be appropriate. Such an event might warrant further investigation in order to characterize and understand it. Depending on the nature of the event, rapid communication by the trial sponsor to other parties (e.g., regulators) might also be warranted.
Question Text: [Is/Was] this event of special interest?
Mapping Instructions: Does not map to an SDTM variable. The SDTM annotated CRF indicates that this field is NOT SUBMITTED.
Implementation Notes: This CDASH field may be used just to trigger other CRF pages, or to populate a value in AECAT or AESCAT. If submitted, this information could be submitted in a SUPP--.QVAL dataset where SUPP--.QNAM= "AESI" and SUPP--.QLABEL = "Adverse Event of Special Interest". Refer to the current SDTM and SDTMIG for instructions on placement of non-standard variables (NSVs) in SDTM domains.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication whether an adverse event required medical or surgical intervention to preclude permanent impairment of a body function, or prevent permanent damage to a body structure, due to the use of a medical product.
Question Text: Did the adverse event require intervention to prevent permanent impairment or damage resulting from the use of a medical product?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: This criteria is used for SAEs associated with a medical product (e.g., device). Sponsors should follow requirements for the reporting of adverse events involving medical devices to health authorities.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Any serious adverse effect on health or safety or any life-threatening problem or death caused by or associated with a device, if that effect, problem, or death was not previously identified in nature, severity, or degree of incidence in the investigational plan or application (including a supplementary plan or application), or any other unanticipated serious problem associated with a device that relates to the rights, safety, or welfare of subjects.
Question Text: [Is/Was] the adverse event a unanticipated adverse device effect?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: The assessment is about a device identified in the data (i.e., which has an SPDEVID). The device may be ancillary or under study.

CDASH Variable Label: Rel of AE to Device-Related Procedure
DRAFT CDASH Definition: The investigator's opinion as to the likelihood that device-related study procedures caused the AE.
Question Text: What is the likelihood that device-related study procedures cause the adverse event?
Prompt: Rel of AE to Device-Related Procedure
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: The relationship is to a device-related procedure where the device is identified in the data (i.e., which has an SPDEVID). The device may be ancillary or under study.

CDASH Variable Label: Rel of AE to Non-Dev-Rel Study Activity

Question Text: What is the likelihood that a non device-related study procedure cause of the adverse eventt?
Prompt: Rel of AE to Non-Dev-Rel Study Activity
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: The relationship is to a protocol-specified non-device-related activity where the device is identified in the data (i.e., which has an SPDEVID). The device may be ancillary or under study.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The plan for subject continuation to the next phase of the study or another study at the time of completion of the CRF.
Question Text: Will the subject continue?
Mapping Instructions: This information could be submitted in a SUPPDS dataset as the value of SUPPDS.QVAL where SUPPDS.QNAM= "DSCONT" and SUPPDS.QLABEL="Subject Continue". Refer to the current SDTM and SDTMIG for instructions on placement of NSVs in SDTM domains.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Identifies the study epoch or new study in which the subject will participate.
Question Text: What [is/was] the next [Period/Epoch/Trial] the subject will [continue to/enter/enroll]?
Mapping Instructions: This variable does not map to an SDTMIG variable. The annotated CRF indicates that this field is NOT SUBMITTED.
Implementation Notes: Typically, this is used as general prompt question to aid in monitoring, data cleaning, and subject tracking. This information could be submitted in a SUPP--.QVAL dataset where SUPP--.QNAM = "--DSNEXT" and SUPP--.QLABEL = "Next EPOCH". Refer to the current SDTM and SDTMIG for instructions on placement of NSVs in SDTM domains.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An indication the subject's treatment information was revealed to any unauthorized site personnel during the trial.
Question Text: [Is/Was] treatment (unblinded/unmasked) by the site?
Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTM variable. If the CDASH field DSUNBLIND = "Y", then the SDTMIG variables DSDECOD and DSTERM = "TREATMENT UNBLINDED" and DSCAT = "OTHER EVENT". If DSUNBLIND = "N", then the CRF should be annotated to indicate that this value is NOT SUBMITTED.
Implementation Notes: There may be multiple rows in the SDTM DS dataset to represent this information, each with the appropriate DSCAT values. One row could indicate the treatment was unblinded (using DSCAT= "OTHER EVENT") and another row could indicate the status of the subject at the end of their participation in the trial (using DSCAT = "DISPOSTION"). If DSUNBLND is "Yes" and information was collected about the reason for the unblinding, populate DSCAT with "OTHER EVENT" and the SDTMIG variables DSTERM with the free text and DSDECOD with the sponsor-defined standardized text (e.g., TREATMENT UNBLINDED). If DSUNBLND is "Yes", and the unblinding also resulted in the subject discontinuing the trial prematurely, populate DSCAT with "DISPOSTION" and use the SDTMIG variables DSTERM and DSDECOD to capture the applicable discontinuation details. If the unblinding occurred due to an adverse event, DSTERM contains the text of the adverse event. In the AE domain, the SDTMIG variable AEACNOTH ("Were any other actions taken in response to this adverse event?" ) may include text of "Treatment Unblinded". DSUNBLND may also be used to document intentional unblinding at a protocol-defined point in the trial.

Implementation Notes: This is not related to the trial's condition. It cannot be a value of "PRIMARY DIAGNOSIS" or "SECONDARY DIAGNOSIS". The event date type may be the date of diagnosis, symptoms, relapse, or infection.

Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTM variable. For the SDTM submission dataset, concatenate all collected CDASH DATE and TIME components and populate the SDTM variable RFICDTC in ISO 8601 format.
Implementation Notes: This will be the same information on informed consent used in the SDTM Disposition domain.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Minute informed consent signed, in an unambiguous time format (e.g., mm).
Question Text: What minute did the subject sign informed consent?
Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTM variable. For the SDTM submission dataset, concatenate all collected CDASH DATE and TIME components and populate the SDTM variable RFICDTC in ISO 8601 format.
Implementation Notes: This will be the same information on informed consent used in the SDTM DS domain.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A subject's date of birth (with or without the time of birth). The complete Date of Birth is made from the temporal components of Birth Year, Birth Month, Birth Day and Birth Time..
Question Text: What [is/was] the subject's date of birth?
Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTM variable. For the SDTM submission dataset, concatenate all collected CDASH DATE and TIME components and populate the SDTM variable BRTHDTC in ISO 8601 format.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The age of the subject, expressed in AGEU.
Question Text: What [is/was] the subject's age?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: If age is collected, it should be collected as a number and, to be correctly interpreted, the age value should be associated to a variable for the age unit. It may be necessary to know when the age was collected, as an age may need to be recalculated for analysis, such as deriving age at a reference start time (RFSTDTC for SDTM). BRTHDTC may not be available in all cases (due to subject privacy concerns). If age is collected, it is recommended that the date of collection also be recorded, either separately or by association to the date of the visit.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Units of time that are routinely used to express the age of a person.
Question Text: What [is/was] the age unit used?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: If age is captured on the CRF, the age unit must be known to make the age value meaningful. The age unit might be collected on the CRF, in those cases where the protocol allows for any age group, or it may be preprinted on the CRF (typically with the unit of "years").

DRAFT CDASH Definition: Sex of the subject, as determined by the investigator.
Question Text: What [is/was] the sex of the subject?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Collect the subject's sex or gender, as reported by the investigator. This is a phenotypic assessment, not a genotypic assessment.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An arbitrary classification based on physical characteristics; a group of persons related by common descent or heredity (US Centers for Disease Control).
Question Text: Which of the following 5 racial designations best describes you? (More than one choice is acceptable.)
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: When collecting data on race of subjects, use the US FDA's 5 designations for race (American Indian or Alaska Native, Asian, Black or African American, Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander, White).a If collecting data outside the US, the recommendation is to use the same categories, except for Black instead of Black or African American. If multiple races are collected, an alternate sponsor-defined variable structure is required. Sponsors may record multiple self-reported races for a subject by appending a suffix to denote multiple collected races (e.g., RACE1, RACE2) and populate RACE with the value "MULTIPLE". Sponsors should refer to the FDA guidancea; see the SDTMIG for additional information.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: An arbitrary classification based on physical characteristics; a group of persons related by common descent or heredity (U.S. Center for Disease Control).
Question Text: Which of the following racial designations best describes you? (More than one choice is acceptable.)
Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTMIG variable. This information could be submitted in a SUPPDM dataset as the value of SUPPDM.QVAL when SUPPDM.QNAM = "CRACE" and SUPPDM.QLABEL="Collected Race". See SDTMIG for additional information.
Implementation Notes: Use CRACE when more detailed race categorizations are desired (e.g., more than the 5 designations for race used by the FDA). The HL7 race vocabulary tables are recommended, as these are designed to collapse up to the SDTM variable RACE with CT (e.g., American Indian or Alaska Native Asian, Black or African American, Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander, White). When collecting data outside the US, the recommended categories are the same except for Black instead of Black or African American. If multiple races are collected, an alternate sponsor-defined variable structure is required. Sponsors may record multiple self-reported races for a subject by appending a suffix to denote multiple collected races (e.g., CRACE1, CRACE2) and populate CRACE with the value "MULTIPLE". If sponsors choose to map the extended Race codelist values to the RACE CT (e.g., Japanese to Asian), then this mapped variable would be reported using the SDTMIG variable RACE. Sponsors should refer to FDA guidance.a

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A social group characterized by a distinctive social and cultural tradition maintained from generation to generation, a common history and origin and a sense of identification with the group; members of the group have distinctive features in their way of life, shared experiences, and often a common genetic heritage; these features may be reflected in their experience of health and disease.
Question Text: Do you consider yourself Hispanic/Latino or not Hispanic/Latino?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: Use when values are being collected using the exact non-extensible ETHNIC codelist (C66790) values. Sponsors should refer to the FDA guidance regarding the collection of ethnicity.a

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A social group characterized by a distinctive social and cultural tradition that is maintained from generation to generation. Members share a common history, origin, and a sense of identification with the group. They have similar and distinctive features in their lifestyle habits and shared experiences. They often have a common genetic heritage, which may be reflected in their experience of health and disease. When submitting to the FDA, the collected values must be rolled up to the permissible values in ETHNIC.
Question Text: What [is/was] the ethnicity of the subject?
Mapping Instructions: This information could be submitted in a SUPPDM dataset as the value of SUPPDM.QVAL when SUPPDM.QNAM = "CETHNIC" and SUPPDM.QLABEL = "Collected Ethnicity". Refer to the current SDTM and SDTMIG for instructions on placement of non-standard variables (NSVs) in SDTM domains.
Implementation Notes: Use when values are collected using the NCI Thesaurus codelist for Ethnicity As Collected (C128690), the extended HL7 hierarchy of codelist values, or other regulatory agency-specific controlled terminology for ethnic group. Sponsors may append a suffix to denote multiple collected ethnicities (e.g., CETHNIC1, CETHNIC2).

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The age of the subject at study start, expressed as a range.
Question Text: What [is/was] the subject's age (range)?
Mapping Instructions: Maps directly to the SDTM variable listed in the SDTM Target column.
Implementation Notes: If a numeric age value is available, then populate the AGE variable instead. Either the AGE or AGETXT field should be populated, not both. See the CDASHIG for more information.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: The age of the subject at study start, expressed as descriptive text and not a range.
Question Text: What [is/was] the subject's age (description)?
Mapping Instructions: If the collected value is a range, then the result maps directly to AGETXT. If the collected value is a description, the result maps to SUPPDM.QVAL where SUPPDM.QNAM = "CAGETXT" and SUPPDM.QLABEL = "Collected Age Text".
Implementation Notes: If only collected age ranges (e.g., 0-3, 18-25, >65) are expected, those should be collected using AGETXT. If collecting age descriptions (e.g., neonate, adolescent, adult), those must be collected using CAGETXT.

DRAFT CDASH Definition: A free-text field to be used when none of the pre-printed values for RACE are applicable or if another, unprinted selection should be added to those preprinted values.
Question Text: What was the other race?
Mapping Instructions: This does not map directly to an SDTMIG variable. This information could be submitted in a SUPPDM dataset as the value of SUPPDM.QVAL where SUPPDM.QNAM = "RACEOTH" and SUPP.QLABEL="RACE OTHER". See also SDTMIG Section 5.2, Demographics (DM). Refer to the current SDTM and SDTMIG for instructions on placement of NSVs in SDTM domains.
Implementation Notes: When creating the Demographics form, it is suggested that the 5 standard race categories be included. Sponsors may include another value of Specify, Other with a free-text field for extending the list of values. The RACEOTH variable contains the free-text added by the site. The value(s) added in the optional variable might or might not collapse up into one of the 5 categories specified by the FDA Guidance.a See the SDTMIG for additional information.
